question: Read the following situation: In music, a kindergarten teacher plans an experience to work on different musical elements. The first activity involves having the children listen to the sound generated by a dripping tap and then reproduce it through claps. Next, they must repeat the activity by listening to the ticking of a clock. What element of musical language is being worked on with the previous experience?
options: Pulse
Accent
Duration
Intensity

question: With the intention of characterizing the main economic transformations in Chile after the Great Depression of capitalism, a teacher began the class by asking her students: "How was the 1929 crisis confronted in Chile?" Based on their responses, she noticed that the tendency was to argue that the import substitution industrialization (ISI) model was the solution to the crisis. Hence, in order to change this preconception, she handed out the following text for them to analyze: According to figures provided by Gabriel Palma, despite that by 1935 the mining sector - especially nitrate - had not yet recovered to the levels of 1929, nitrate production had increased from 694,000 metric tons in 1932 to 1,200,000 tons in 1935; in the same period, refined and blister copper production grew by 270%. In contrast, although agriculture had already recovered its 1929 production levels by 1933, it subsequently stagnated [...]. If at the end of 1932 there were 129,000 unemployed workers, the number fell to 69,000 at the end of 1933, to 28,000 by the end of 1934, to 8,000 a year later, and to less than 5,000 in 1936. 20th Century Chilean history, Correa, S What preconception of the students is the teacher trying to change with the analysis of the previous text?
options: To think that the economic recovery began with the creation of CORFO
To suppose that CORFO promoted the modernization process of the copper activity in Chile
To understand that the objective of CORFO was to eliminate the negative effects in the nitrate industry
To believe that due to the creation of CORFO the Chilean state ended economic dependence

question: A teacher in a 2nd year of secondary education has been working with the students on the following objective: "Determine the inverse function of a linear function," and will conduct a final assessment to verify their learning regarding the addressed content. Which of the following evaluation indicators directly reflects the achievement of this objective?
options: They create value tables for the inverse of a linear function
They calculate the equation associated with the inverse of a linear function
They identify the domain and range for inverse functions of given linear functions
They solve problems that involve the application of the inverse of a linear function

question: Read the following situation: An educator invites children to play with changing letters in some words to form others. She instructs them: If we change the letter "g" in the word "gato" (cat) to the letter "p", "gato" becomes "pato" (duck). Then she invites them to repeat this exercise with different words. What metalinguistic skill is the educator working on with this activity?
options: Lexical awareness
Syllabic awareness
Syntactic awareness
Phonemic awareness

question: To work on the concept of hydrostatic pressure, a third-year high school teacher plans a class with the objective for students to apply the concept of density when comparing different bodies and substances. At the start of the class, an activity will be conducted to introduce the concept of density. Which of the following tasks should students perform, after the initial activity mentioned above, in order to address the objective of the class?
options: Predict what will happen when three immiscible liquids are poured into a graduated cylinder, and explain why the observed phenomenon occurs
Determine experimentally the density of a liquid based on the pressure it exerts on the bottom of the container holding it
Explain how the melting of Antarctic icebergs affects sea level, based on the density of ice and liquid water
Relate qualitatively and quantitatively the density of different bodies with the same mass and different volume, or the same volume and different mass

question: In the context of the unit "Youth Issues and Contemporary Media", conducted in a 2nd year class, a teacher plans for the class to learn about the stencil technique and then apply it in a visual project based on their personal imaginations. To do this, the teacher decides to use an educational resource that allows presenting the discussed technique to the students. Which of the following options proposes an educational resource that would address the stated objective?
options: An original graphic piece made with stencil
Video about the techniques used by Keith Haring in his works
Documentary about the work and technique used by the visual artist TAKI 183
Tutorial on street interventions by artists who practice wild style

question: Read the following situation:   In the 2nd grade of a school with an Integrated Educational Support Program, four students are attending who have been diagnosed with Language Development Disorder, who still show a significant number of phonological simplification processes, as expected for their age. The classroom team observes that, for example, in tasks involving dictation, these students tend to confuse certain phonemes, such as: /p/ and /b/.   According to the above, which of the following activities addresses the support requirements presented by the students?
options: Hear and repeat words with the phonemes /p/ and /b/
Listen and discriminate between the phonemes /p/ and /b/
Attend to and produce pseudowords with the phonemes /p/ and /b/
Observe and perform exercises to produce the phonemes /p/ and /b/

question: In a higher middle level, a group of children is playing in the sandbox. Sara piles up sand, adds some rocks to the sides, and runs a car through it. Then she says, "I made a very big hill and the road is dangerous, just like the one near my house!"  In relation to the core of Understanding the Sociocultural Environment, which of the following learning objectives is achieved in Sara's performance?
options: Recognize ways of life in the community
Identify protection and safety norms
Describe common activities in her community
Distinguish elements of the natural and cultural landscape

question: In a 3rd year secondary class, within the unit "Instrumental Ensemble", a teacher plans classes with the objective that students establish their own rules for composition. Which of the following sequences of activities that the students will carry out is the most appropriate to work towards the objective set by the teacher?
options: They generate ideas of rhythmic patterns, identify their metric characteristics, and overlay melodies and harmonies, choosing those that best fit the rhythmic design. Subsequently, they assemble the piece, correct errors, and present their work to their peers, who comment on the final results

They individually present their ideas to the rest of the class. They choose the idea that best represents the class and their musical tastes. Then they practice and perform it as a group. Subsequently, they analyze the result by making improvements for a final presentation

They pose a challenge and generate the first ideas that allow them to focus and plan the continuation of the work. Subsequently, they conduct experimentation and sound exploration as a first proposal, which they review, improve, and share around feedback from peers and the teacher

They listen to a piece chosen by the teacher, analyzing the elements of formal construction. They practice the different sections on their instruments and perform the piece, which they later use to create their own versions, respecting the formal structure of the proposed model

question: A preschool teacher is working on the project "The emotions that live in me". This project includes the learning objective from the Artistic Languages core: "To express emotions, ideas, and experiences through art by experimenting with pictorial, graphic, and modeling resources". Throughout the project, they have conducted different experiences and, to conclude, the teacher invites the children to present the creations they have made during this project.  While Martina presents her work, she says: "I made two balls, one yellow and one green, because they are my favorite colors". The teacher asks: "What emotions does your painting express?". Martina responds: "I don't know".  Which of the following teacher's interventions is relevant to help Martina achieve the learning objective?
options: Excellent, Martina! I see your work represents a game with colored balls. Do you like playing with balls? Why is one ball green and the other yellow? Do they remind you of any emotion?
Very good job, Martina! The balls you created are in the colors we use to identify emotions. The green represents calm, and the yellow represents joy
Great job, Martina! You selected your favorite colors for your work. Can you tell me why you drew balls? What do you feel with the colors green and yellow? Why are they your favorites?

Well done, Martina! But would you like to complete your work remembering you need to represent some experience and emotion? It could be the joy of playing ball with your friends or the sadness when you lose the ball

question:
Amparo, from preschool, plays with spoons of different sizes during zone play. During the game, when arranging them in a row, the girl says: "Oh! It's like a family, this is the dad because it's the largest, this is the mom because it's medium, and this is the son because it's the smallest." The educator has observed her several times engaging in this type of mathematical play.
What mathematical thinking skill underlies Amparo's performance?
options: Comparison and patterns
Comparison and serial order
Grouping and correspondence
Grouping and sequential order

question: A teacher is working with a middle school class on the unit: “The body as a reference for design” and wants to evaluate the objective: “Experiment with various languages, techniques, and modes of artistic and visual creation”. Which of the following indicators should be present in the guideline that will evaluate the stated objective?
options: Draws sketches representing themes linked to the body as a support
Conducts bibliographic research on different concepts linked to the body as a support
Creates a prototype or clothing model that addresses the issue of the body as a support
Artistically investigates the materiality for the creation of a clothing prototype

question: Read the following situation:  A class team of 7th grade, in the subject of Language and Literature, will work on understanding informational articles and reports. To do this, considering the characteristics of the class and the students who present learning disabilities in reading, the special education teacher suggests including in the teaching planning the principles of Universal Design for Learning concerning the engagement of students and the various forms of action and expression. In addition, he suggests developing the executive function of planning in the students.  Based on the above, which of the following strategies considers the suggestions of the special education teacher?
options: Show the topic of the text through a conceptual map, ask students to reflect in groups, and conduct an individual self-assessment at the end of the activity
Contextualize the topic for the students through a video, images, and a brief text, present the theme through a dramatic work, and propose a group goal for the development of the activity

Choose a topic of interest to the students, present the reflection on what they read through a written text, song, or poem, and make a list of actions they need to develop to carry out the reflection work

Discuss the proposed topic, ask students to make an explanatory video in which they must conceptually define the terms they are working on in class, and present the video to the class incorporating metacognitive questions

question: Read the following situation: Two children are playing and both are speaking at the same time, expressing their thoughts, although without addressing each other. According to J. Piaget, which cognitive development stage is manifested by the language presented in the above situation?
options: Preoperational
Sensorimotor
Formal operations
Concrete operations

question: According to Piaget, what is the significance of symbolic play in the cognitive development of a child?
options: It involves, through action, developing the ability to adapt to the environment, strengthening their motor skills
It involves, through stacking objects, the development of spatial concepts, cause and effect, and perceiving properties of objects
It involves, through imitation, developing the ability to distinguish the imaginary from the real, consolidating their own mental representations
It involves, through the acceptance of rules, the development of skills such as problem-solving, reasoning, and strategy analysis

question: Read the following definition: It is the first form of play for human beings. They are usually individual games that consist of repetitive muscular movements. What type of game does the previous definition correspond to?
options: Constructive
Symbolic
Functional
Parallel

question: What evaluation activity allows connecting a musical activity with other areas of artistic expression?
options: Body percussion rhythmic sequences associated with a listening
Perform a prose narration based on instrumental music auditions
Perform with recycled material instruments repertoire from different folk traditions
Perform musical pieces from different visual notation methods

question: A teacher is working with an 8th grade class on the unit Listening, Singing, and Playing. Then, she will evaluate the following learning objective: "Interpret repertoire related to the music listened to; mainly considering the development of the skill of fluency of phrasing and dynamics".  What indicator reflects the achievement of this learning?
options: They sing and play paying attention to the beginnings and endings of a work, interpreting it with musical sense
They sing and play in more than one voice, considering rhythmic aspects specific to the work and style
They sing and play a repertoire performing phrasing typical of the style of the interpreted work
They sing and play applying breaths and intensity regulators in the interpreted work

question:
Read the following situation:
Sofia is in 2nd grade at a special school, she is nine years old and in the subject of Language and Communication, in the reading axis, she can identify her written name and the initial sound of words when they begin with vowels; in the oral communication axis, she can understand simple instructions and short texts that are read to her, verbally express herself to respond with words like yes or no, and those that refer to basic needs of food, hygiene, and health, accompanied by gestures. Her oral expression is supported with a communication board. Her teacher has proposed to work on the following learning objective: Demonstrate comprehension of the read narratives, extracting explicit and implicit information. The evaluation will consist of making an oral recount of the story.
Considering the stated learning objective, which of the following curricular adaptations is the most appropriate to respond to the support needs that Sofia presents?
options: Provide a set of images of the story, so she can recount it from beginning to end
Ask closed questions that aim at the recap of what was narrated, whose answers are yes or no
Offer paper and pencils so she can illustrate what was narrated, then questions oriented to the recount of the story will be asked
Include in a group of peers, in a dramatization activity of what was narrated, asking them to act out the story from beginning to end

question: What reference is fundamental to determine mental health from a psychosocial perspective?
options: The symptoms of a mental illness
The perception of one's own efficacy in the world
The adaptability of the individual's behavior
The conditions and environments that shape the life experience

question: Read the following situation: In a second-year secondary music class in the symphonic music unit, a group of students listens to Ravel's Bolero. The teacher guides the listening towards the various ways of orchestrating its generating motif. What activity is most appropriate for students to develop a critical reflection on the work heard?
options: Analyze the musical motif on which the composition is based
Evaluate the expressive effect generated in this work from the timbral element
Relate the compositional procedure to its historical period
Distinguish the instruments that participate in the work and their interventions

question: Read the following situation: A teacher shows 8th grade students the evaluation guideline that will be applied to evaluate the following objective: “Interpret contemporary visual manifestations, taking into account the characteristics of the medium of expression, materiality, and visual language.” Which of the following options includes an indicator that should be present in the evaluation guideline?
options: Describes the emotions expressed in the work
Expresses their personal appreciation of the technical procedures used in the work
Establishes relationships between textures, the techniques used to create them, and the meaning of the work
Relates symbolic elements present in the work to the author's biography

question: In an 8th grade class, within the framework of the unit "Design and Nature," the teacher presents the following objective: "Create designs of objects that reflect inspiration from nature." Of the following evaluative activities, which one allows the teacher to verify the achievement of the objective?
options: Observe the relationship between a bird and an airplane, and design an infographic that describes the relationship of form and structure between them
Observe elements of nature that contain symmetry, and design a presentation demonstrating the direct relationship with design elements
Observe design objects inspired by nature, and design a timeline that reflects the changes it has undergone from its creation to the present
Observe the relationship between design objects and nature, and design an object that mimics the form or structure of an element of nature

question: Read the following situation:  When evaluating a four-year and ten-month-old student, the institution's speech therapist observes the absence of the phoneme /m/ in her speech and a deviation of -2 SD in vocabulary on the TECAL test. Additionally, the special education teacher notes that her sentences lack structure, but her productions are still intelligible. Therefore, the classroom team meets to determine in which area they will start providing support to the student.  Based on the above, in which area should support be initiated to favor the student's language development?
options: Lexical evocation
Phonological awareness
Phoneme articulation
Sentence structuring

question: In the context of an assessment, a teacher asks their students to analyze the order of the rules of good thinking that Descartes presents in "Discourse on the Method." Which of the following options is an appropriate evaluation indicator for the specified activity and consistent with the expected learning?
options: They justify the hierarchy of the method's rules
They identify the first cause of each of the rules
They recognize each of the rules in the Cartesian method
They explain the differences between each of the rules of good thinking

question: In the context of planning a class on the endocrine system, which of the following contents is necessary for your students to know in advance so they can differentiate how hormones initiate the response in the target cell?
options: Hormone-releasing factors
Chemical nature of hormones
Humoral regulation of hormones
Feedback mechanisms of hormones

question: A teacher in a kindergarten class has decided to work with the children around the following objective from the Sociocultural Environment Understanding Core: "To understand the roles played by family and community members and their contribution to the common well-being." For this purpose, the teacher proposes that the children draw what they enjoy doing with their family and then discuss the roles each member plays in that situation. When presenting her work, Martina shows her family vacation and comments: "Here I drew when we went to the beach with my mom and grandma, here I am playing with all my cousins and my dog. We slept in a tent and had a barbecue."  Which of the following teacher interventions guides the girl towards achieving the proposed learning objective?
options: Your drawing is very complete, Martina. Congratulations! Could you tell us who prepared the barbecue and who set up the tent?
Very good, Martina! Great detail in your drawing of what you all do. What activities do you like most during your vacation?
It is a very detailed drawing. Good job, Martina! Can you tell us what other delicious things your mom cooked? Were you able to help her?
Excellent drawing, Martina! We can clearly see the family members. What other activities do you do during your vacations?

question: Read the following situation: Josefa forms two rows with five plastic soldiers each. The educator approaches and asks her: "Where are there more soldiers?" After she responds, the educator gathers more soldiers from one of the rows and asks her: "Where are there fewer soldiers?" What logical-mathematical notion does the educator want to verify in the previous situation?
options: Seriation
Transitivity
Conservation
Reversibility

question: In a 1st Year secondary class, to address the following objective: "Determine needs or opportunities in the establishment, which involve creating a service by collecting, systematizing, and interpreting pertinent information to propose corresponding solutions," the teacher plans to use various instruments with her students such as surveys, data tables, and graphs, among others, to collect and systematize the information. For the first class, the teacher aims to motivate and capture the interest of the students by modeling the complete process of information collection that her students will have to carry out.  Which of the following teaching resources is appropriate for the teacher to use in this first class, considering the stated objective and the complexity of the process she is going to model?
options: A digital wall, indicating the processes of joint and collaborative construction of the instruments, and the analysis and interpretation of the results based on tables and graphs

A flowchart, indicating the different moments and relationships between the processes required for the construction and application of instruments, and the analysis of results based on tables and graphs

A PowerPoint presentation, showing different types of instruments for data collection, their characteristics, the basic principles for their construction, and examples of data systematization in tables and graphs

A tutorial video, describing the process of developing and applying instruments to collect information in a fictional situation, and then how it is possible to interpret this information based on tables and graphs

question: Read the following situation: A teacher who teaches 6th grade classes classes is teaching the technique of xylography. She has shown various references and explained the carving and printing process to her students. With this knowledge, she instructs them to draw a possible sketch to be made under the characteristics of this engraving technique. When she begins to tour the room to observe the students’ progress, she realizes that some designs contain words. The teacher pauses the class, takes one of these sketches as an example and explains what happens with the printing if the word is carved into the matrix as it is in the drawing. What pedagogical action would guide the students to correctly create the print matrix?
options: Transfer the drawing to the matrix using carbon paper
Show the printing of a xylograph to explain the result of the carving
Put a mirror on the sketch so that the image is seen in reverse
Draw a letter on the blackboard to explain the different planes in the creation of depth

question: A secondary teacher in 4th grade, while planning the unit on acid-base reactions, sets the following learning objective: “Relate concentration to the mathematical expression to calculate pH in different types of weak acids and bases”.  Which of the following prior knowledge is essential for students to have acquired to achieve the proposed learning?
options: Relationship of the strength of the acid with its conjugate base
Representation of the dissociation of electrolytic substances
Expression of the equilibrium constant of a chemical reaction
Determination of molarity in electrolytic chemical solutions

question: While working with students on the topic of electrical circuits, a teacher notices that one of the students believes that when connecting several identical bulbs in series, the first will shine more than the last because the current 'depletes' as it flows through each successive bulb. Which of the following teacher actions would directly challenge this student's preconceptions?
options: Ask the student to explain in their own words what happens to the current intensity and potential difference in a series circuit
Conduct a demonstration experiment where the student can observe the light intensity of bulbs when connected in series
Ask the student to measure and compare the current intensities of each bulb connected in series and explain the observations
Show a circuit with four bulbs connected in parallel and another with four bulbs connected in series and describe what is observed

question:
Read the following situation: 
A 1st year Middle School teacher plans an activity where his students will create a painting directly outdoors, taking Impressionist movement as a reference.
What prior learning, referred to pictorial procedures, is necessary for these students to manage?
options: The use of juxtaposed spots to create color mixtures
The application of non-realistic colors to the different elements of the landscape
The application of local color in the forms as the first layer
The use of black color to represent the shadows

question: A 12th-grade teacher is planning a class with the objective of "Determining the graphical representation of the inverse function of an invertible function presented graphically". Which of the following skills should students have achieved to meet the proposed objective?
options: Solve algebraic expressions
Graph functions from tables of values
Perform reflections of figures regarding an axis
Perform reflections of figures regarding a point

question: During the unit The Democratic Break, a teacher in a second-level high school class plans to work on the analysis of iconographic sources. Therefore, she has prepared different questions for her students to interrogate the following photograph:  (Photograph of one of the murals of the Ramona Parra Brigade painted in October 1972 and intended to defend the Popular Unity. This photograph was exhibited at the Museum of Contemporary Art. Later rescued in 2002 by Eduardo "Mono" González.)  Which of the following questions is directed at conducting an internal analysis of the photograph as a historical source?
options: Does the source have bias?
What is the central idea conveyed by the source?
What is the historical context of the source?
Who is the author of the source?

question: A teacher plans to conduct a formative assessment with first-year secondary students to measure the achievement of the goal to evaluate the cultural impact of globalization from their everyday experience. Which of the following evaluative activities should the students perform?
options: A debate about the effects of globalization on the city they live in
A brainstorming session to temporally contextualize the globalization process
A role-playing game on the importance of technological innovation in communications
An essay on the impact of adopting foreign customs by their family in recent years

question: In the 5th grade of a school for deaf students, Macarena attends, who recently integrated into the course two years ago, as she previously attended a school with special educational needs. Although Macarena can now communicate fluently and adequately in Sign Language, she still faces challenges in writing, finding herself at a level well below the class average.  Based on the above, which of the following strategies is most appropriate to promote the natural development of writing in Macarena?
options: Model the student's natural writing using a Communication Notebook
Reinforce writing through the creation of stories related to topics of interest to the student
Practice writing through the creation of texts supported by verb and vocabulary notebooks
Encourage writing based on sentences made in sign language by the student and written by the teacher

question: The 8th-grade students are working on an anthology of comic strips, selected by themselves, which do not have titles. The teacher requests that they carry out the following activity: Read the comic strips, establish the relationship between the iconic and verbal resources, and then write a title for each comic strip. What learning is promoted by the strategy of titling each comic strip?
options: Determine the theme of the story
Identify the narrative character of the story
Discover the logical sequence of the narrative
Describe the narrative purpose of the story

question: A 7th-grade teacher is developing the unit on the emergence of the first civilizations. In one of the classes, the goal is addressed: “Understand the political organization of ancient Egypt.” One of the activities requested of students is reading a source of information about how the political authority of the pharaoh in Egypt was configured, to then evaluate in accordance with the proposed goal.  Which of the following performances would demonstrate the achievement of the goal by the students?
options: They describe the process of unification of the kingdoms of Egypt, which strengthened the central government
They distinguish the form of organization of the most powerful in the kingdom, who chose the first among their equals to assume power
They recognize the actions of the priests, who, in order to build the pyramids and worship their gods, gave power to a noble
They explain how the invasion of other peoples, to obtain the lands of the Nile, led to the rise to power of a general who managed to defeat them

question:
Read the following situation: 
In a 4th Grade class, the teacher conducts the following activity with their students in a Mathematics lesson:
1. They form bundles of 10 ice cream sticks, which they tie with red tape.
2. Then, they form groups of 10 with the bundles of sticks tied with red tape. These are tied with green tape.
3. Finally, they form a new group of 10 with the groups tied with green tape. These are tied with blue tape.
At the end of each step, the teacher asks: "How many ice cream sticks do they have tied up with tape?".
What objective does the previous activity aim to develop?
options: Understand that the action of joining is associated with addition
Understand the positional value of the digits in a number
Understand how the decimal numbering system is constructed
Understand how a number is decomposed according to positional value

question: Read the following situation:  Isidora is a student with expressive language disorder who attends preschool at a school with special educational needs. The special education teacher has suggested that the students play professions and trades. Isidora has chosen to be a doctor, and the first thing she does in her role is ask the nurse for the equipment to attend to her patient, which she does through gestures and phrases, such as: "that one that goes here (pointing to the heart) and listen".  According to what is described, what psycholinguistic skill is diminished in Isidora?
options: Definition
Association
Integration
Nomenclature

question: A preschool teacher plans to work with her group on the following learning objective: "To discover, in playful contexts, phonological attributes of known words, such as identifying final sounds." Which of the following learning experiences is the most appropriate to work on the objective set by the teacher?
options: Guess words that will be mentioned phoneme by phoneme
Discover words that form assonant rhymes within a poem
Physically represent the number of syllables in different words
Identify words that have the same sounds in different alliterations

question: A group of 8th-grade students is preparing the setup of an interactive timeline on the evolution of cameras. Before carrying out the exhibition, they realize that they need to build a module to support the cameras they will display. As they have no experience in building a solution of this type, they turn to the Technology teacher for guidance. Which of the following pedagogical actions would enable the students to achieve the objective?
options: Request the design of the device and provide a list of materials and tools that their students will need, along with assembly instructions for the device
Recommend that students only use images of the objects and create a simpler presentation made with materials they are familiar with
Help students determine the strength of the materials they intend to use and indicate safety measures for working with tools, especially if they are dangerous
Co-design with students, considering the presentation and the objects they want to display, to devise an action plan for the construction of the device

question: A teacher is working on the portrait unit with the students in a 7th grade class. To complete the unit, she will ask them to draw a portrait of a classmate. What exercise should the students perform before taking on the challenge?
options: Perform linear valuation and hatching exercises
Reproduce the lights and shadows reflected on a classmate's face
Copy the parts of the face separately, such as the nose and ears
Draw the outline of a face and place its parts proportionately

question: A teacher has worked with students in class on the objective: "Understand the concept of percentage variation."  Which of the following indicators should the teacher consider when evaluating the learning worked on, as it reveals that the objective is being achieved?
options: Identify percentage increases or decreases in contextualized situations and record them symbolically
Solve problems involving calculating percentage increases or decreases in real situations
Compare percentage increases or decreases in contextualized situations to guide decision making
Recognize the data that allows solving problems involving calculating percentage increases or decreases

question:
A 3rd grade teacher wants to start the unit on Greece by introducing his students to the geographical environment in which this culture developed and analyzing the influence it had on it.
What type of map would be most suitable for the objective set by the teacher?
options: Physical
Political
Economic
Climatic

question:
A teacher designs an evaluation instrument to assess the creation of a Stop Motion. 
Which of the following indicators shows that students are adequately addressing the
creation process by using production strategies specific to this technique?
options:
Creates sketches of objects or characters, incorporating them into a story using
comic strips

Takes photographs of objects or characters in a continuous sequence, slightly changing their
positions
Records various scenes from different cinematic angles
Presents their video in digital format made with various filmic shots

question: A teacher is working on the inferential level of reading comprehension based on the reading of the following short story: Vacations Next year my family plans to go to the moon. My mother told me to invite a friend if I wanted, of course, to not get bored she must think. The truth is that I'm not very excited, I don't know, I've never liked going off the planet much, I prefer to eat noodles with sauce and cheese. Anyway, my friend says she would love to go, but tells me she needs a new bikini, they say women on the moon are very beautiful, but I don't believe it. We will stay at the Hotel Armstrong and travel on Pullmanmoon (hopefully it has a bathroom). I'll bring a melon. Catalina Yáñez What question could the teacher ask the students to promote reading at the inferential level?
options: What does the protagonist feel about the trip to the moon?
What should a vacation trip to the moon be like?
Why does the protagonist's mother tell her to invite a friend?
What attitude does the protagonist take regarding the trip at the end of the story?

question: In a class corresponding to the topic of chemical equilibrium, a high school teacher is working on the learning objective "Explain the state of equilibrium reached by chemical reactions". During the activity, the teacher notices the confusion of the students regarding the following question they asked: "Do reactants and products reach the state of equilibrium separately?" Which of the following activities would allow the teacher to refocus the teaching-learning process for the students?
options: Model the progress of a reaction until half of the products have been formed
Model the macroscopic and microscopic characteristics of a chemical system until it has reached equilibrium
Perform an experimental procedure that shows the influence of factors that disturb the state of equilibrium in chemical systems
Perform an experimental procedure that allows predicting the concentrations at equilibrium of the participating species, based on their initial concentrations

question: A teacher is working in an ethics class on the notion of Aristotelian virtue. She asks her students to provide examples of actions or situations where virtue is present. A student states that "someone who studies a lot is as virtuous as someone who does a lot of sports." Which of the following teacher interventions would allow the student to rethink her statement to advance towards a correct understanding of Aristotelian virtue?
options: The teacher reminds the student what the cardinal virtues are
The teacher asks the student to explain what she understands by the golden mean
The teacher guides the student to remember that the human will tends towards the good
The teacher asks the student to consider whether athletes have innate skills to do the sport they like

question:
Read the following situation:
A teacher working with a 7th grade student has started working with a student who has intellectual disabilities. The major challenges for him are related to short-term memory, sustained attention and planning, while difficulties are also observed in relation to reading and writing skills. Considering the multidimensional approach and the characteristics of the student described in the context, which dimensions are most relevant to consider when designing learning experiences?
options: Context and participation
Participation and adaptive behavior
Intellectual skills and participation
Intellectual skills and adaptive behavior

question: In a special school, the 8th grade teacher works daily on executive functions in a transversal way to promote the development of her students' cognitive skills related to the control and self-regulation of their own behavior, thereby preparing them for the next year when they will face a work level that has greater demands. Therefore, she carries out the following sequence of activities: 1. Generation of random numbers and letters: one by one, each student verbalizes aloud, in any order, numbers from 1 to 20 or letters of the alphabet, without repeating letters or numbers that have already been named by their classmates beforehand. 2. Category repetition: the teacher reads a list of 20 words and asks the students to hit their table when they hear the name of an animal. After reading, she asks them to repeat all the words that are not animals and then the ones that are. 3. Visuospatial routes: the teacher shows a 3 x 3 grid matrix (3 squares on each side), indicating the square where the game begins. Then, the matrix disappears, and the students must give the visuospatial instructions such as: move up one square, advance to the square on the right, to indicate the square of the initial stimulus. To which executive function do the activities implemented by this teacher correspond?
options: Planning
Inhibitory control
Mental flexibility
Working memory

question: A fifth-grade teacher, in the unit Characteristics of today's technological objects, proposes to students to carry out a debate about programmed obsolescence and its relation to sustainability. After the results of the debate, he notices that a group of students associates the concept of programmed obsolescence with product quality. Considering the students' level, which of the following explanations from the teacher would help clarify this concept?
options: It is the determination or programming of the end of the useful life of a product or technological object, and it is not related to its quality, as it happens when a more advanced product or one with new functions is released to the market

It is the determination, during manufacturing, of how long a product will last, to force its renewal at the end of the period. It is related to quality when the product develops faults and malfunctions after a time

It is the period of time calculated in advance by the manufacturer in the design phase of a product, for it to expire and become unusable, without affecting its quality, as it happens when a more advanced product is released to the market and people replace the one they already have

It is a determination achieved by various means, for example, due to lack of spare parts or the need to buy a new replacement product, by the natural evolution of products and people's desires to change what they already have, unrelated to its quality

question:
Read the following situation: 
At the start of a thematic unit, a teacher explains the concept of habituation through a definition. In this context, a student asks for clarification through an example.
Which of the following statements represents an appropriate example to explain the concept of habituation?
options: If we repeatedly put ice on our hand, it will become numb and we will not feel the ice as cold
We generally buy the same shampoo because we have become accustomed to it
By lying repeatedly, we generate distrust in others
If a car parked on the street sounds its alarm every night at 2 a.m., at some point we will stop caring

question: A student is presented with the following problem: DDT was a widely used insecticide; however, after some years of intensive use, it lost its effectiveness on insects. Why did DDT stop being effective on insects after intensive use? The student responded as follows: After a period of long exposure to DDT, the insects developed defenses that allowed them and their offspring to be immune to this insecticide. What erroneous preconception appears in the student's response?
options: The individuals in a population that have advantageous structures or processes will survive and leave offspring
The individuals in a population that acquire advantageous mutations will survive and pass on this mutation to their offspring
The individuals in a population, in order to adapt to the environment, must develop structures or processes that allow them to survive and leave offspring
Most individuals in a population die when competing with those that present advantageous variations, with the latter being the ones that will leave offspring

question: Read the following situation:  Jaime is a student with developmental disabilities attending 5th grade. This student finds abstract tasks that are distant from his everyday life difficult. The classroom team is preparing the activities for the national holidays and considers the learning objective: Recognize various expressions of the cultural heritage of the country and its region.  Which of the following strategies diversely addresses Jaime's support needs to achieve the objective proposed by the classroom team?
options: Visualize, in educational capsules, typical Chilean games according to the region of Chile
Prepare the presentation of typical Chilean legends and later present it to their families
Work collaboratively organizing the monuments and historical sites of our country in posters
Participate in the planning and subsequent execution of typical dishes from their locality, to then present them at a stand

question: Second-year secondary students, guided by questions from the teacher, are examining classic experiments that provide evidence about the location of genetic material and its relationship to phenotype in eukaryotes. From the nuclear transfer experiment in Xenopus laevis by John Gurdon, which provided evidence that intestinal epithelial cells contain all the genetic information necessary for the complete development of an adult organism, one of the students asks: “Why are enucleated oocytes used in the experiment, and not any other enucleated cell?”. Which of the following explanations by the teacher allows for a precise conceptual response that is understandable to the student’s concern?
options: Because for the process of nuclear transfer to generate a complete organism, the recipient cell of the nucleus must be undifferentiated and must contain differentiation factors in its cytoplasm that are distinct from those of the nucleus donor cell

Because an oocyte is an undifferentiated cell that has the potential to give rise to more than one cell type, as its cytoplasm contains differentiation factors that selectively activate the transcription and translation of the genes present in the transplanted nucleus

Because other types of body cells, like intestinal cells, are differentiated cells whose cytoplasm contains differentiation factors that stimulate the removal of genes that are necessary for the complete development of an embryo, which does not occur in an oocyte

Because the cytoplasm of an oocyte, unlike that of other differentiated cells, such as intestinal cells, has all the differentiation factors necessary for the activation of specific genes contained in the transplanted nucleus, which allows for the complete development of an embryo

question: Read the following situation: Amalia is a four-year-old student diagnosed with expressive language disorder, who attends preschool at a language school. Her teacher highlights her interest in participating in class; however, in her language development, it is observed that she does not manage to adapt to the needs of the interlocutor nor respect turns in conversation. Furthermore, when she speaks, she omits the phoneme /r/ within the word, does not mention semantic categories and still does not articulate passive and conditional sentences. Considering Amalia's age, in which dimension of language is her support need appreciated?
options: Semantics
Phonological
Pragmatic
Morphosyntactic

question: A teacher will begin working on the unit 'Economic models and their impact on the territory', with the goal of having her high school students analyze the transformations experienced by the geographical space as a result of the relationships between economic, demographic, and spatial processes. Therefore, she has decided to start the class by recalling the meaning of the term geographical space, through a group brainstorming session. In the responses given by the students, those that characterized the geographical space exclusively from its physical or natural component prevailed, attributing to it an attribute of stability in the medium and long term. Considering the didactic principles that guide the teaching of geography, which one of them is essential to work on in order to expand the concept of space that this group of students has?
options: Connection
Location
Interdependence
Causality

question: A fourth-grade teacher is planning a class in which she will work on comparing decimal numbers. To start, she will ask the students to compare the following numbers: 1.37 with 1.4 and 1.25 with 1.5; for this purpose, she is looking for a type of representation that will help them perform the exercise and understand the content. Which of the following activities would meet the requirement that the teacher has set?
options: Write the quantities as hundredths
Express the quantities in fractional form
Represent the quantities with concrete materials
Illustrate the quantities in everyday situations

question:
Read the following text: 
Language is yet another manifestation of the human capacity for symbolization. For it to develop, maturity of mental and psychological processes is required.
Which theory of language development is related to the previous text?
options: Social
Innatist
Cognitive
Behaviorist

question: A teacher of a 7th grade class, in the context of a unit with the following objective: "Propose solutions of repair, adaptation or improvement", takes their students to visit the school's storage room, where technological equipment and furniture that has been decommissioned is stored, with the purpose of carrying out a project of repair, adaptation or improvement of a technological object. Considering the teacher's objective, what action should the students take first to carry out their repair project?
options: Review and test the available equipment and objects, and analyze the possibilities for repair
Determine the necessary actions for the repair or adaptation of the objects and equipment of the school
Select the reusable materials available at the school to make repairs on the objects
Define which tools they will use for the adaptation and repair tasks of the objects that can be reused

question: Read the following situation:  Claudio, a student diagnosed with specific language disorders, attends 3rd Grade in a school with a inclusion programme. His classroom team points out that he shows important progress in the area of reading, especially in decoding. Nevertheless, he still requires support to understand written texts, as he fails to extract relevant information; for example, he cannot understand written instructions during evaluations. Although the team has implemented various diversified strategies, they consider it necessary to make curricular adjustments when conducting written tests.  Based on the above, which of the following curricular adjustments is appropriate to make with the student?
options: Organize relaxation spaces during readings
Adjust the student's response medium to oral form
Provide more time during written evaluations
Highlight key concepts in the presentation of the evaluation

question:
A high school teacher works with students on reading, analyzing, and interpreting lyrical texts related to the theme of freedom. In an activity, they listen to the song “El cautivo de Til Til” and are then asked to analyze and characterize the lyrical speaker based on the following excerpt:
They say Manuel is his name
and they take him on the road to Til Til,
that the governor does not want
to see his genteel bearing along La Cañada.
They say that in war he was
the best and in the city
he dazzled like the lightning
of freedom.
(...)
They say he was like lightning
when he rode his steed
and that as the rider passed
all called him by the name Manuel.
I don’t know if I will return
to see him free and genteel,
I only know he smiled on the way
to Til Til.
El cautivo de Til Til, version by Aquelarre.
Upon reviewing the activity, the teacher comes across the following student response:
“The speaker seems to be reflecting and remembering to himself. He is somewhat sad and melancholic, but he is brave, like the lightning of freedom. This comparison made by the speaker seems very interesting to me because lightning is something that dazzles and so is freedom. He mentions the name of a place in Chile, and in general, he does not use difficult words.”
What problem does the student's response present in relation to the purpose of the activity proposed by the teacher?
options: It shows a partial confusion between the subject dealt with and the voice expressed in the text
It develops a response that strays from what was asked in the activity, as it focuses on other elements of the poem
It focuses on personal appreciations over the content of the lyrical text to characterize the speaker
It shows a lack of relevant references to the text in the response, as it only refers to a comparison made in it, but not with other verses

question: A teacher of a 3rd grade set as a goal in a class that their students answer statistical questions from random dice games. For this, they formulated questions that could only be answered through experimentation. Which of these questions, formulated by the teacher to the students, involves conducting a random experiment to answer it?
options: If you roll the die twenty times, how many times do the opposite faces sum to seven points?
If you roll the die thirty times, how many times do six points appear on the top face?
If you roll the die twenty times, what is the maximum number of points the top faces can sum to?
If you roll the die thirty times, what is the possibility that the top faces sum to less than thirty points?

question: In the context of problem-solving with affine functions, what is the most common misconception students have?
options: The variables are related directly proportional, just like in the case of linear functions
The slope of all affine functions is positive because they are graphed in the first quadrant of the Cartesian plane
The variables are related inversely proportional, unlike what happens with linear functions
The intercept with the y-axis of all affine functions is positive because they are graphed in the first quadrant of the Cartesian plane

question: Read the following situation:  In a school for deaf individuals, the 4th  grade is working on the subject of text production in the students' second language , therefore the special education teacher proposes as an activity the group creation of a farewell letter for a teacher who is finishing her professional practice and leaving the institution.  Based on the above, which of the following strategies is the most pertinent for the special education teacher to develop the activity?
options: The special education teacher presents to the students a text she has prepared for the farewell letter addressed to the other teacher. Along with this, she warns them that this text contains errors and that they must correct them together, pointing out the mistakes made and proposing corrections for each of them

The special education teacher has her students individually come to the front of the class to narrate in Sign Language what they want to write in the farewell letter to the departing teacher. Then, they discuss what they have proposed and choose the five ideas they find most suitable for the entire class. After this, they begin to write the text together

The special education teacher asks her students to individually come to the blackboard and write down one idea for the farewell letter to the other teacher. Once each student has added an idea to the list, the teacher begins to correct them one by one, marking the errors and changing or adding the correct elements in each of the sentences

The teacher asks her students to each narrate in Sign Language what they want to write in the farewell letter to the other teacher. As each student recounts their idea, the teacher writes it in Spanish on the blackboard. Afterwards, they choose the three ideas that most of the class likes and the teacher connects them, drafting the letter

question: A teacher of 8th grade is working with her students on a project that involves building a model of house lighting. Upon examining the students' work, she notices that one group has not succeeded in making the LED lights in the different rooms turn on and off independently. Which of the following actions by the teacher would help this group of students understand how to construct the circuit they need?
options: Show them that the connection between the lights must be in parallel for them to be independent. Then, ask them to make the connection diagram and replicate it in the model circuit

Explain the characteristics of series and parallel circuits, indicating that in the former, the electrical current follows a single path, so the lights are not independent. Then, ask them to connect the lights in parallel and test the result

Look at the circuit diagram they built with them and explain that for the lights to function independently, they must be connected in parallel. Then, ask them to redo the electrical diagram by connecting all the lights from the source and rebuild the circuit

Explore with them how the lights behave when connected in series and parallel when turning some on and off. Then, analyze the circuit diagram they built to determine the changes they will make. Finally, ask them to connect all the lights in the model according to the new diagram

question:
A kindergarten teacher for a younger middle level plans to work on the area of verbal language and also reinforce fine motor skills. To do this, she conducts the following educational experience: The children, guided by a story, pretend to be pirates searching for hidden coins in the room, which they then must put through the slot of a treasure chest. The teacher observes that Amanda kept her coins in her pocket, so the teacher asks her why she didn't put them in the treasure chest, and she replied: "I can't put the coins in because they fall." 
What intervention by the teacher would allow Amanda to receive formative feedback to achieve the learning objective?
options: "Amanda, hold the coins between your fingers and I will help you insert them." Guide her hand to insert some coins. Then ask: "Can you try it by yourself?"
"Amanda, would you like to try with another piggy bank? I have some with larger slots and that could be easier. Would you like to try again? I'll give you more time for it."
"Amanda, do you need help? Would you like someone to show you how to insert the coins? Look at that classmate, he is doing it correctly, stand next to him so you can try it again."

"Amanda, can you show me how you are doing it? Why do you think you can't put the coins in? Could you do it another way?" After listening to her answers, model the manipulation of the coin and practice together

question: A teacher of 8th grade is starting a project with their students with the objective of providing a service aimed at the elderly in a home sponsored by the school. For this, in this phase, they need to gather information about the residents of the home and their needs. Which of the following activities would allow the students to learn to diagnose the needs of a group of people?
options: Develop a problem tree to identify the negative situation, relating its possible causes and effects
Compare different methods of collecting information, such as interviews and questionnaires, and establish their advantages and disadvantages
Research the SWOT matrix and complete a guide on a case, identifying strengths, opportunities, weaknesses, and threats
Brainstorm possible problems that might be affecting them, and then investigate methods to collect information

question: Read the following situation:   Juan is a student with intellectual disabilities in 1st grade in a special education school. In class, he is observed to be very attentive and motivated to learn; however, his speech is unintelligible to those who do not know him well yet. He understands images and pictograms and is starting to read. The teacher believes it is necessary to complement the student's speech to provide him with options for responding to activities.   Considering this information, what technological resource supports achieving the proposed objective for the student?
options: Application that contains pictograms and photographs to set up communication boards, Let me talk
Application with a word processor that allows simultaneous writing of text and pictograms, Araword
Program aimed at supporting reading and writing learning at home, Learning to read with Bartolo
Program that works on different language skills (reading, writing, and oral communication), Leo Primero

question: A high school teacher is preparing a class on Kantian theory about the possibility of knowledge; to do this, they must determine the prior learning experiences necessary for their students to adequately address such a theory. What prior learning should the teacher recognize in their students to tackle this theory?
options: Recognize the ideas of D. Hume and J. Locke as theoretical antecedents to the proposition of I. Kant
Understand English dogmatism and empiricism as currents preceding the Kantian proposal
Identify the notions of time and space as structures of thought necessary to know, according to I. Kant
Understand the concepts of phenomenon and noumenon as Kantian explanations about what can be known

question: A teacher is working with his students on the concept of tangible movable heritage. To facilitate understanding, he shows them various images that exemplify the concept. Which of the following options contains a correct example of tangible movable heritage?
options: Photographs of parks and nature reserves
Images of houses declared national monuments
Vessels from an ancient culture
Legends and stories of oral tradition

question: What is the most suitable activity to introduce perspective by stimulating the creative thinking of students in the pseudonaturalistic stage (12 to 14 years old)?
options: Ask the students to draw an element of the landscape that they can see from a window. Then, have them step away from the window, draw the same element, and compare the sizes obtained in both drawings

Ask the students to make a copy of a painting that contains a landscape in perspective with two vanishing points, and then ask them to compare their drawing with the original painting, considering the perspective
Ask the students to draw a horizon line, establish two vanishing points, and then project a cube and compare the different figures obtained with their classmates
Ask the students to prepare an exhibition in which they explain the laws of using the horizon line based on images of their interest

question: What skills are primarily developed in boys and girls through rule-based play?
options: The symbolization of real situations
Empathy and conflict resolution
The socializing elements of agreements and taking turns
The learning of socially established practices and roles

question: Read the following situation:  Gabriela is a 5th-grade student with a reading learning disability who has difficulties with reading comprehension, attention, and concentration. Her challenges are mainly perceived in the subject of Language and Communication, where she easily gets distracted, talks while the teacher explains, and does not finish the activities.  The classroom team, after using diversified strategies and providing personalized support, decides to implement a curricular adjustment for access, linked to the organization of the environment. As one of the educational responses to address her challenges, Gabriela was placed in a strategic location within the classroom, given that her behaviors interfere with her learning and that of her classmates.  Based on the above, which of the following options justifies the decision of the classroom team?
options: Enhances essential adaptation skills and self-directed learning that are critical for curricular advancement
Recognizes the individual and contextual characteristics of the student valuing the diversity present in the classroom
Reduces barriers to participation and access to information, without lowering the student's learning expectations
Strengthens social skills that affect the student's participation and develops autonomy in her learning process

question: A teacher in a Third-Year class was working with the students on the exponential function and in the next class will work on the logarithmic function. To do this, she will carry out a guided activity in which, starting from the algebraic expression f(x) = log10(x), they will tabulate some values for x with their evaluation in this function and will sketch its graph, determining its domain and range through an algebraic analysis. For this reason, the teacher decides to complement the algebraic analysis using another methodological strategy.  Considering this pedagogical purpose, which of the following strategies carried out by the teacher promotes the understanding of the domain and range of logarithmic functions?
options: Pose situations modeled by logarithmic functions expressed algebraically, interpreting, together, their domain and range concerning the given context
Project the graphs of different logarithmic functions, each one with its respective inverse exponential function, determining the domain and range of the first, based on the second
Pose the algebraic expression of different logarithmic functions, asking the students to obtain their corresponding domain and range, expressed in mathematical notation
Project the graphs of different logarithmic functions, each one with its respective algebraic expression, analyzing, together, the differences and similarities observed in their domain and range

question: Read the following situation: A special education teacher receives a new student, gathering important information that will allow him to better address their learning processes. Thus, he identifies that the student faces challenges related to sustained attention and short-term memory, as well as in integrating into collaborative work groups. According to the Multidimensional Approach, what are the two dimensions that the teacher should address to identify the student's challenges?
options: Context and participation
Adaptive behavior and participation
Intellectual skills and participation
Intellectual skills and adaptive behavior

question: In most school texts, in the study of electricity and direct current electric circuits, the "hydraulic" analogy is used to promote the understanding of concepts such as current intensity and electric potential, among others. In this analogy, the characteristics of a basic electric circuit are compared to the characteristics of a flow of water circulating through a pipe, as if the flow of water represented the moving charges.  Which of the following options corresponds to a limitation of this analog model as a teaching strategy for teaching direct current electric circuits to students?
options: It is not valid for studying series or parallel circuits
It encourages students to believe that electric current can "go down" through a wire, but not "up"
It does not allow students to associate the concept of resistance in an electric circuit with any observable property of the hydraulic circuit
It promotes students to think that current intensity in an electric circuit is equivalent to water flow in a hydraulic circuit

question: A second-year secondary teacher, in the unit of Kinematics, will conduct a diagnostic assessment to understand the students' conceptions about the motion of objects in free fall.  According to this purpose, which evaluative activity will allow the teacher to obtain the best evidence about the students' prior knowledge on this content?
options: In an outdoor space, they throw various objects upwards and compare the parameters of each throw
They answer questions aimed at comparing and anticipating the time it will take for various objects to fall from a certain height
They indicate in a drawing what the direction of the acceleration of a tennis ball is when it is in the air from one player to another
They create a conceptual map that incorporates kinematic concepts related to the motion of objects in free fall

question: Consider the following situation: A teacher presents the following text to their 1st year high school students: One of the main problems that the European Union faces is that its refinery units are outdated and less sophisticated compared to the latest generation plants operating in Asia and the Middle East. The latter have drastically increased the amount of petroleum products flowing to global markets, and European companies find it increasingly difficult to compete with them. To these circumstances, it is added that the strict environmental regulations of the EU pose a challenge to the region's refineries... a fact exemplified by the very strict environmental regulations in force in Spain, which led to the closure of the Cepsa refinery in Tenerife three years ago. Cepsa claims that the EU regulations make each barrel refined at their two operational plants in Spain incur an additional cost of approximately two dollars, which they consider a burden difficult to bear, while feeling the focus of attacks by activists, who accuse them of not respecting environmental standards. (Source: Econoticias, Energías Renovables www.chiledesar rollosustentable.cl) Based on this resource, what question could the teacher pose to promote cognitive conflict in their students regarding technological solutions and their impact?
options: What economic impact could the lack of promotion of technological innovation at the fossil fuels industry level have for the European Union?
What factors underlie the greater efficiency and competitiveness of refineries in Asia and the Middle East?
Should the legal frameworks of the European Union relax existing regulation, in order to create better conditions for the development of its oil industry?
What relationship can we find between the situation described in the text and the situation of the energy industry in our country?

question: A preschool teacher plans to evaluate the following learning objective from the Verbal Language core: "To express oneself orally in a clear and understandable manner, using complete sentence structures, appropriate and precise verb conjugations with respect to tenses, persons, and communicative intentions." To this end, she designs a learning experience in which the children must create a story from a sequence of three images, which will be recorded by the teacher. Isabel, age 5, narrates the following story: "Once upon a time, there was a boy who had a llana (frog) and a pello (dog) and the boy had fallen asleep on the bed and the llana (frog) got out of the jar and was gone, and he caught it. The end."  Considering the expected performances in expressive language for a child of this level, what feature of Isabel's story shows that she has not yet achieved the indicated learning?
options: She does not include adjectives in the sentences
She does not include adverbs in the sentences
She does not conjugate verbs in the past perfect tense
She does not correctly articulate the trilled 'r' sound

question: A 4th grade classroom team must plan an activity in the Mathematics subject that works on the Learning Objective: "Measuring lengths with standardized units and making transformations between these units." Considering, on one hand, the lack of motivation towards learning that the students have shown and, on the other, the ease that the class has to work collaboratively, the team decides to carry out a group activity outside of the classroom where the students must choose polygonal objects found anywhere in the school, measure them, and record their lengths to then analyze the information and make transformations between the measurement units.  According to the above, which of the following options justifies the strategy implemented by the classroom team?
options: Strengthens the self-directed learning of the students through contextualized teaching situations
Enables the involvement of the students in the task, through the diversification of learning situations
Enhances the development of language and symbols related to geometry through group and contextualized work
Develops self-regulation of the students' behavior through the diversification of learning situations

question: In a 6th grade class, within the goal of forming relationships between variables using mathematical language, a teacher also aims to promote the development of inquiry skills. Considering the level at which they will be performed, which of the following activities allows achieving both objectives?
options: Continue the sequence 98, 94, 90, ..
Build a sequence where adding five is used
Describe the relationship between the numbers in the columns. 14, 27, 310, 413
Describe the relationship between the numbers in the columns. 123, 145, 257, 279

question: Students in a 3rd year class must represent the historical trajectory of the Contemporary Era according to the temporal criteria defined by historian Eric Hobsbawm, by constructing a timeline. To accomplish this task, the teacher provided the following instruction: 'Highlight the segment that represents the "long 19th century" and the segment corresponding to the "short 20th century".' However, upon reviewing the responses, many were incorrect, as seen in the following example: What concepts should be necessarily consolidated in students for them to achieve the objective posed by the activity?
options: Milestone and conjuncture
Continuity and change
Historical time and chronological time
Background and consequences

question: A teacher will start working with her 6th grade students based on the following plan:  6th Grade Unit: The political organization of Chile and the basic principles established by the Constitution. Learning Objective: Explain some fundamental elements of the democratic organization of Chile, including the division of state powers and their representation through elected officials (councilors, mayors, deputies, senators, and President). Evaluation indicator: Recognize the main characteristics that a representative democratic regime must have.  Which of the following contents is essential for the students to manage in order to achieve the expected performance?
options: Citizen participation
Rights and duties
Type of State
Method of appointing authorities

question: Read the following situation: Members of an educational community do not read the boards or bulletin boards where relevant information for them is published, for this reason, time and resources are wasted delivering the information repeatedly. In Technological Education, a group of students is challenged to face this problem within their school and propose a technological product to solve it. They begin to develop the different stages of the project methodology; in one of them, they focus on analyzing different written communication media. To which stage of the project methodology does this activity correspond?
options: Information search, after identifying the need
Solution design in light of research results
Communication of solution designs
Evaluation of the solution and functional tests

question: A teacher wants to encourage her students to read poems by focusing on the use of language. She explains that a characteristic of poetry is to find new ways of saying things, and that for this, the poet creates poetic images, which correspond to the literary figure of the metaphor. Which of the following verses allows the teacher to exemplify this literary figure?
options: They told me that in the Upside Down Kingdom the bird swims and the fish flies, that cats don't meow and say yes because they study a lot of English. Maria Elena Wash
The dove got confused. She was confused. By going north, she went south. She thought the wheat was water. She was confused. Rafael Alberti
Butterfly of the air, how beautiful you are, butterfly of the air, golden and green. Federico García Lorca
There is the flower tree, butterfly in suspense beauty distributed, in all directions. Robinson Saavedra

question: A teacher of a 12th grade class has asked the students to relate environmental pollution with acid rain. Which of the following activities should they work on to correspond to a scientific inquiry?
options: Conduct bibliographic research on the relationship between acid rain and environmental pollution
Formulate a question that considers the relationship between acid rain and environmental pollution and answer it through an experiment
Conduct a practical laboratory work that shows the relationship of environmental pollution with acid rain
Practically demonstrate the effect of acid rain on vegetation

question: A 6th-grade teacher will evaluate their students with the following objective: "Contrast existing technological solutions and identify the needs they responded to in the context in which they were developed." Which of the following student performances allows for demonstrating achievement of the objective proposed by the teacher?
options: They contrast various solutions corresponding to the same group of given needs
They compare needs that have arisen in different areas of social development and their changes over time
They establish similarities in the historical contexts in which technological objects of the same class were conceived over time
They compare the needs underlying the creation of the same type of technological objects according to the era in which they were created

question: A teacher who has covered the Electricity unit with a 5th-grade class will conduct an assessment; among other things, she hopes to verify the achievement of the following indicator: "Distinguish materials that are conductors from those that are insulators of electricity".  Which of the following activities addresses the previously stated indicator?
options: Ask students to observe a working electric circuit and compare it with another that did not work and explain why the latter does not function

Ask students to observe a chart showing the conductivity percentage of different metals, and then answer the following question: Which of the metals on the chart would serve best as a conductor in an electric circuit?

Ask students to observe an image showing a man handling electric wires with rubber gloves, so they can then answer the following question: Why must the man use rubber gloves when handling electric wires?

Ask students to observe the image of an incomplete electric circuit and then select from materials, such as a metal fork, a plastic comb, and a rubber band, which one or ones could complete the circuit so that the light bulb turns on

question: Read the following situation:  The classroom team of a 2nd grade, in the subject of Language and Communication, is carrying out the following activity:  1. Shared reading 2. Questions to the students 3. Emilio's response  The Monkey Tito   Tito is not an ordinary monkey. Tito does not like to climb trees and hates to eat bananas. He prefers to stroll, smell the flowers, and collect the nuts that fall from the trees.  According to what has been presented, what is the support requirement that Emilio presents?
options: Locate information about the characters in a text
