Locate details about the events in a text
Draw conclusions from explicit information in a text
Establish time and place relationships between the actions in a text

question: In a summative assessment in 8th grade, during a collective instrumental execution session, the objective is: “Create and perform a song with a strophic structure,” a teacher prepares the students to present their creations. Which of the following evaluation indicators reflects achievement of the objective?
options: They create a song in which a melody is repeated with various variations
They create a song in which a melody is repeated identically with different lyrics
They create a song in which a new contrasting melody follows the main melody
They create a song in which a coda or final segment is added at the end of the main melody

question:
Read the following situation: 
At the end of the unit "The Legacy of Athens," one of the objectives that the teacher sought to evaluate was understanding the main characteristics of Athenian democracy, and to check the level of achievement, he asked his students to construct a comparative chart between Athenian democracy and current Chilean democracy. To evaluate the responses, he prepared a guide based on the elements that have historical continuity and those that do not.
Which of the following evaluation indicators evidences the recognition of an element of change between both democracies?
options: Identifies the presence of institutional hierarchy
Recognizes the differentiation in the functions of the government
Identifies the level of participation in government decisions
Recognizes the right to citizenship subject to the fulfillment of requirements

question: A first-grade teacher is designing a group activity to work on the skill of exploring along with the class objective: "Identify the senses and their function." To begin the activity, she will give each group a banana, an orange, and a pear, and will ask them to smell, look at, touch, and taste each fruit. Considering the above situation, which of the following activities could the teacher include next so that the students achieve the stated objective?
options: In a worksheet listing the characteristics of each fruit, mark the answers to questions that can be answered using the senses: Which fruit is yellow? Which fruit is sweet? Which fruit is rough? Which fruit is fragrant?

In a worksheet where characteristics that can be perceived with some of the senses (sweet/sour, yellow/orange, soft/hard, pleasant smell/unpleasant smell) are indicated, classify the fruits according to these criteria

In a worksheet where each sense is represented with an icon, mark the answers to these questions: With which sense did you realize that the banana is yellow? With which that the pear is sweet? With which that the banana peel is smooth? With which that the orange is fragrant?

In a worksheet showing the images of these fruits with columns next to them containing a happy face and a sad face, paint your answers to these questions: When eating an orange segment, did you feel pleasure or displeasure? When seeing the color of the banana, did you like it or not? When touching the peel of the pear, did you feel pleasure or displeasure? When smelling the orange, did you like it or not?

question: Which of the following activities promotes the development of empathy in preschool children?
options: Do an artistic activity in pairs, in which both children start a drawing and then exchange it to finish it
Ask them to develop an object in groups with waste materials, and then ask them to describe what was most difficult for them and why
Do a role play in which the children represent a family member during a recreated lunch and point out how they felt impersonating them
Ask the children to perform the story "The Selfish Giant," in which each child assumes the role of one of the characters in the story

question:
A 12th-grade teacher, in the Ethics unit, will work with his students on the concept of Spinoza's moral relativism. 
What notion from Spinoza is it essential for the students to have acquired to understand his moral relativism?
options: Virtuous life
Basic affects
Intellectual love
Active emotions

question: Read the following situation: A Music teacher in a 6th grade, in the unit "Symphonic music", wants to carry out an activity that allows to develop the following objective: "To express, showing increasing degrees of elaboration and detail, the sensations, emotions, and ideas suggested by the music listened to and performed, using various expressive means (verbal, corporal, musical, visual)". What activity carried out by the students meets the above objective?
options: Recreate in the classroom some scenes from a well-known movie after listening to its soundtrack
Represent a sound environment based on a poem
Design choreographies that demonstrate the mood variations experienced after listening to a musical piece
Compose musical fragments that graph the sensation or emotion conveyed by various scenes from a movie

question:
Read the following activity: A teacher proposes to the children to mark different paths on the floor to move from one end of the room to the other, indicating the starting and finishing points. After marking them, the teacher invites them to observe the paths and asks: “Which of these is the longest?”. She then asks them to verify their answers using non-standard units of measurement, such as steps or tiles.
What logical-mathematical skill is the teacher working on with this activity?
options: Grouping
Estimation
Conservation
Orientation

question: A 1st year secondary teacher is working on Darwin's theory of evolution. One of the objectives is for students to understand that species that are more closely related evolutionarily share a common recent ancestor. During a formative assessment, she notices that some students still do not understand this idea. Which of the following activities would allow these students to achieve the proposed goal?
options: Classify organisms by morphological similarities
Answer questions based on the analysis of a cladogram
Construct a diagram categorizing examples of direct and indirect fossils
Compare the vestigial structures of different organisms to establish their kinship

question: Read the following case:  The classroom team in a 2nd Grade is working with its students on the process of acquiring reading and writing through the global method, which favors the recognition of identical elements in words and sentences. However, some students at the level have faced challenges in the "elaboration" phase, specifically in recognizing phonemes and syllables of words.  Considering the global method, what strategy promotes the learning of reading and writing in the students indicated in the previous case?
options: Project sentences that present identical syllables for them to copy in their notebooks
Ask targeted questions so that they frequently use the words under study
Label the objects in the classroom with their names so that they read their syllables emphasizing the sounds
Show cards with various words for them to recognize the equal parts that those terms present

question: Read the following situation: A teacher in a 6th grade, within the unit on Latin American rhythms, wants to address the following Learning Objective with students: 'To improvise and create music by applying experiences and knowledge based on given instructions.' Which of the following indicators allows evaluating the aforementioned objective?
options: They perform rhythmic patterns typical of a given musical genre
They interpret diverse instrumental and vocal repertoire following unconventional scores
They accompany a melody with instruments created by themselves, following a score
They accompany rhythmic sequences with body percussion, organizing the given rhythmic patterns as they choose

question: A third-year high school teacher is designing a written test to evaluate the rotational kinematics unit. If one of the evaluation indicators is "They use the mathematical relationships that describe uniform circular motion to solve various problems," which of the following items is most appropriate to evidence their achievement?
options: Determine the average angular speed of a carousel that makes 5 complete rotations in one minute
Determine how many rotations and how many degrees correspond to a rotation of \( \frac{5\pi}{2} \) radians
Calculate the tangential speed of a comet when its distance to the Sun is \( R \), if its tangential speed was 100 m/s when the distance was \( \frac{R}{2} \)
Express an equation that allows calculating the tangential speed from the angular speed, specifying what each term corresponds to

question: Read the following situation: A 4th grade teacher asks students to bring examples of proverbs from their homes. When the class begins, he explains that proverbs are short texts that can use rhyme and that represent some aspect of common sense or popular wisdom. The students present some of the proverbs they brought, such as: "A gift horse should not be looked at the teeth", "In the house of the blacksmith, wooden knife", and "A bird in hand is worth more than a hundred flying." Then, in pairs, the students make cards of the proverbs they brought from home, discuss the meaning of each one, and finally, each pair explains their proverbs to the class. What aspect of reading is the teacher mainly addressing with this activity?
options: Interpretation of figurative language
Relationship between a text and everyday life
Analysis of the context of proverb production
Understanding contributions of popular culture

question: In the unit 'Understanding Contemporary Artists and Recreating Their Works', in a 4th grade secondary classroom, a teacher is working with her students on the first activity, which consists of a group research project whose objective is: "Investigate the formal qualities and contexts of production of artistic expressions from contemporary art collectives." At the end of the research, the teacher will conduct a formative assessment of the projects based on a rubric.  Which of the following indicators to include in the evaluation rubric allows verifying the achievement of the stated objective?
options: They critically evaluate the expressive purpose of representative contemporary artworks of the selected collective
They design an artistic project of a contemporary artwork, considering the constitutive qualities of the studied collective
They describe characteristics of contemporary artworks of the chosen collective, linking them with socio-historical variables of the territory
They justify the selection of contemporary artworks from the chosen collective, considering variables such as their interests and relationship with their experiences

question: In which of the following situations is the principle of cardinality being enhanced in young children?
options: They number a row of cars from 1 to 7
They count the total number of elements in a set of blocks
They organize books on a shelf and identify the second and third one
They compare two sets of toys and determine which has more

question: A teacher is evaluating the progress of comparative charts, group-developed by 4th year high school students, on the philosophical proposals of Descartes and Locke. The activity aimed for students to understand and compare the epistemological problem in both authors. In one piece of work, it is noted that students present ideas that correspond to the authors, but a comparison between them is not established. Which of the following suggestions provides information about performance and precisely guides students towards achieving the objective?
options: The ideas presented are appropriate and correct, but require a deeper categorization to improve their work
The information for each author is correct. It lacks organization according to questions or concepts in common but with diverse answers and meanings, to make an adequate comparison
It's a good work. However, to improve the comparison, it is necessary to adjust it more rigorously according to the philosophical perspective of each author
The philosophical proposals of each author are well identified. Now, to make the most appropriate comparison, it is important to organize the concepts with more argumentative precision

question: In Natural Science classes, the 7th-grade students synthesize what has been reviewed in the teaching unit on sexuality and self-care by writing a comment consisting of at least three paragraphs, in which they account for the aspects they consider essential to work on. The classroom team, while monitoring the drafting stage, notices that Eduardo, a student with a reading learning disability, who has low frustration tolerance but stands out for his creativity in writing, continues to face challenges in the repetition of ideas. The special education teacher decides not to interrupt Eduardo's writing to avoid stifling his ideas but knows that she must intervene in the revision stage. Given the above, which of the following strategies is appropriate to work with Eduardo during the writing revision stage?
options: Request that he create a synthesis in just one paragraph of what he has written in his draft
Ask him to identify and list the central ideas of the paragraphs in his draft
Invite him to enclose and list each of the paragraphs present in his draft
Guide him in reviewing the discourse markers he used to organize the sequence of his draft

question: A teacher sets the following learning objective for their class: Explain the relationship between the function of a cell and its parts. Which of the following specific learning objectives proposed for an activity would be consistent with the objective of this class?
options: Characterize the cell as the basic structural element of living beings
Compare the organelles and cellular structures that are present in various cell types
Describe the characteristics of the structures present in a prokaryotic cell and a eukaryotic cell
Relate the different parts of the cell to the contributions of scientists such as Hooke, Leeuwenhoek, Virchow, Schleiden, and Schwann

question: In a Mathematics class, the classroom team of a 3rd year Gastronomy program is working with the students on an activity of proportionality. During the monitoring, the general teacher noticed that a group of students is facing challenges in understanding proportional reasoning, specifically in the equivalence between certain values.  Based on the above, which of the following strategies is pertinent to address the challenges of the students?
options: Pose proportion problems without numbers, using recipe ingredients as concepts
Use concrete materials visualizing the proportions of the real ingredients of a recipe by standardizing them
Integrate the proportion formula routinely, emphasizing the lost value to standardize recipes
Use value tables, observing different relationships between the number of servings and ingredients in a recipe

question: In the context of the unit “Creating Innovative Designs of Objects or Technological Systems”, a group of 6th-grade students has generated a project proposal in which they will use disposable plastic bottles tied with raffia to build a bench in the yard of their school. The justification they provide is that it would be an innovative object because it would be made from an unusual material that would withstand rain and sun. Which of the following comments would allow the teacher to give feedback to the students from the perspective of functionality?
options: “Very good work. You managed to address a relevant and familiar problem for your context. However, you still need to specify the characteristics of the material you will use, such as rigidity and hardness.”

“Very well. You have managed to design an innovative object using recycled material, but you need to consider how you will position and connect the bottles so that it can support the weight of the people who use it.”

“Congratulations. Your project is relevant and feasible to carry out, as it considers a proposal that will help the environment by reducing waste generation, but I suggest checking the accessibility to the space you will use.”

“Good team project. I invite you to compare your project with others of similar characteristics, such as park and shopping mall benches, observing the features that make them more comfortable for people.”

question: A teacher is explaining to his third-year high school students the process of sensory knowledge. Which of the following concepts do students need to have previously addressed to understand the concept of perception?
options: Experience
Sensation
Reality
Attention

question: Read the following situation: An educator gives some instructions to the parents to promote reading and writing in children. Among them, she indicates that they should encourage their children to recognize signs, logos, and labels found at home or on the street, asking them to play at reading these same things. What is the name of the strategy suggested by the educator?
options: Shared reading
Text interrogation
Reading aloud
Emergent reading

question: A teacher intends to address, with a middle school class, the graphical representation of a system of equations. To do this, he wishes to reinforce the basic ideas related to the topic, allowing students a better understanding of the new content. Which of the following activities proposed by the teacher is the most suitable for this purpose?
options: Locate points in the Cartesian plane
Graph lines in the Cartesian plane
Solve systems of linear equations
Identify the slope and intercept of given linear equations

question:
Read the following situation:
The 6th grade classroom team wants to promote student participation in order to demonstrate the learning achieved in a particular subject at the end of the school day. To do this, they asked the students to verbalize what they had learned at the end of the class, but it was always the same children who participated. On another occasion, they used "question sticks," but only some students participated, as the rest claimed not to know the answer or didn't respond due to shyness.
Which technological resource is the most appropriate to encourage communication and participation in this course?
options: Software where each student can hear the instructions individually, as well as find them written, for carrying out activities in the computer lab
Software where the students can enter questions or answers, and at the end of the class, the classroom team can project them, identify errors, and provide feedback
Software where students can answer questions individually and at the end of the class, the classroom team can see a report of the percentage of objectives achieved by them
Software where the teacher projects activities and tasks in the classroom for students to solve individually and compare the correct answers provided as feedback

question: For the Learning Objective of Grade 2 'Determine the length of objects using non-standardized measurement units and standardized units (cm and m)', what activity is most suitable for activating the students' prior knowledge?
options: Have the student compare different measurement units
Have the student measure different objects using a ruler
Have the student compare two or more objects and determine which is longer or shorter
Have the student measure different objects using items from their environment (eraser, sharpener, clips)

question:
The technology and science teachers of a 5th grade have agreed to work in parallel on the following science topic: the growth of a plant. For this, the technology teacher has asked her students to develop an explanatory presentation of this process, in order to reinforce the knowledge acquired in science and, additionally, to create an educational material for the 3rd grade students, who also need to learn this process.
Which of the following digital tools is the most appropriate for developing the presentation, considering the purpose and the level of the students?
options: PowerPoint, using text and images
Movie Maker, recording and editing a video
AnimatorDV, to make an animation of the process
Padlet, publishing useful content to understand the process

question: A kindergarten teacher will evaluate the following learning objective of the Sociocultural Environment Understanding core: "Explore household utensils and technological objects that are part of everyday life, such as: bowls, bottle containers, brooms, spoons, phones, among others, using them progressively in daily situations and games." What performance demonstrates the achievement of the stated learning objective?
options: Babble the names of technological objects like phone and clock
Use household utensils, like a cup and a spoon, to play eating
Touch, manipulate, and make sounds with household utensils, such as bowls and spoons
Organize technological objects, like a cell phone and a telephone, in the appropriate play area

question: During the narrative unit in a 6th grade class, a teacher asks the students to write a short expository text about some animal and its characteristics. One of the students writes the following text: Pandas are cute and chubby. I like them because they are funny and lazy, they like to sleep just like me. The other day I went to the zoo and saw a very cute one, but I felt sorry because it was locked in a small cage and looked sad. Which of the following teacher's comments on the student's text would provide better formative feedback and help guide the student's learning?
options: You wrote a short text, but it's a subjective commentary, not an expository text; it does not adhere to objective information
Review the guide on expository text and summarize the characteristics of this type of text. Then, write a new expository text
Expository texts do not use the first person, nor include adjectives with personal judgments. Your text has personal judgments
You managed to identify an animal. You should write about its characteristics: where do pandas live, what is their anatomy, what do they eat, what size are they?

question: Read the following situation: A teacher is developing activities aimed at enabling students to explain the relationships between the Mapuche and Spaniards during the colonial period. To achieve this, she designs a first activity based on the following text: In the parliament held in Paicaví in 1605, it was established that the frontier people had to pay a moderate tax in kind, personal service being prohibited. If they provided people to work on Spanish estates, they would be paid in silver or its equivalent. Furthermore, they had to go to war against the enemies of the King. The Indians responded satisfied that they would remain at peace, "[...] because His Majesty had addressed the total cause of their rebellion and its remedy, which was the personal service for which they rebelled [...]". It is observed, then, that by these years friendly Indians still came from the sector of the encomienda Indians. They began, however, to outline their specific condition -with respect to the previous century-, since they were no longer Indians who went to war with their encomenderos from the central area of the country, but encomienda Indians from the same conflict area. On the other hand, their situation had been attempted to be alleviated by prohibiting personal service, the main reason for their discontent. The friendly Indians on the Araucanian frontier, (Andrea Ruiz-Esquide). Based on the above text, what activity evaluates if students identify relationships between the Mapuche and Spaniards during the colonial period?
options: They make a poster summarizing the objective of the Paicaví treaty
They summarize from a schematic the main agreements between the Mapuche and Spaniards
They underline all forms of contact between Spaniards and Mapuche and create a thematic card for each one
They locate the parliament of Paicaví and recount how the border between both groups changed over time

question: Which of the following authors proposes in their theory of motor development evolution, 4 phases ranging from the impulsive body to the represented body from 0 to 12 years old?
options: Piaget
Le Boulch
Wallon
Gallahue

question: A 2nd-year teacher, in their upcoming Physics classes, will work with their students on the following objective: "To understand Newton's laws and apply them to explain everyday situations".  Considering the above situation, which of the following options presents two erroneous preconceptions that could directly hinder the learning of the proposed objective?
options: Distance and displacement are synonyms; the force needed to move an object depends on its weight
If a body is at rest, no forces act on it; acceleration always occurs in the same direction as the movement
The position of a body can be described by its distance from the starting point; only living beings can exert force
Friction always opposes movement; the force exerted by a small object on a large one is less than the reaction force of the large one on the small

question: A teacher of a preschool class is developing the unit Thinking like a scientist. The children have actively participated in pedagogical experiences such as conducting experiments and learning about the scientific method. The skills they will practice are observation and inference. Which pedagogical resource would be suitable to promote the development of these skills in the children?
options: Metacognition sheet, to record the new concepts learned during the experiment and then draw conclusions
Tracking guide, to record the initial ideas and observations of the experiment, and then compare the data obtained afterwards
Planning log, through which the steps and initial ideas of the experiment are organized, and then the experience is monitored
Collection and observation tools that facilitate and allow a more precise exploration, to then describe the findings obtained

question: Read the following situation:  In the 1st grade of a school with special educational needs support, three students diagnosed with mixed language disorder attend, who during the first term obtained low grades in the English subject. The teacher points out that the class has approached the language through multiple activities, such as songs, rhymes, games, greetings, object identification, games with colors and numbers, among others, but that the students with mixed language disorders have not managed to reach the minimum learning outcomes of the unit. According to the situation presented, which of the following assessment procedures is appropriate to identify the support needs of the students?
options: Apply a checklist to understand the strategies used by the teacher of the subject
Directly observe to record the skills and challenges of the students in the subject
Carry out a descriptive evaluation scale to understand the interaction dynamics among peers during English classes
Evaluate using a rubric to compare their evaluations and requirements with the students from previous years who achieved the minimum learning outcomes

question: In a 2nd year secondary class, a teacher identifies that, when applying the properties of logarithms, some students develop exercises proposing the following equality: \( \log(a + b) = \log(a) + \log(b) \). Which of the following teacher strategies directly helps students identify their error?
options: Present some numerical counterexamples
Reinforce the practice of operations with logarithms
Review the properties and definitions of logarithms
Express logarithms in power form

question: A teacher worked with students in a 5th grade class on the ability to sing by reading a simple score. Three years later, the teacher returned to teach the same group of students, now in 8th grade, and intends to work on the same content and skill: singing by reading a score. What type of methodological principle related to the domain of musical language is the teacher applying?
options: Associativity: to produce learning, a conceptual musical component must be associated with a skill
Segmentation: a generic musical component is divided into subcomponents that are worked on progressively
Recursivity: the same musical language component is presented again at increasing levels of complexity
Reiteration: the same musical component must be worked on multiple times, first through imitation and then reading

question: Read the following situation:  Asuncian is a four-year-old student who has recently been diagnosed with Autism Spectrum Disorder and has entered a special school. She shows challenges in interpersonal relationships; she responds appropriately to some actions proposed to her, but she is inflexible in the face of changes in spaces and routines. Her mother reports that it is very difficult to leave home with Asuncian to visit someone, go to the park, or shop, as she constantly cries, so her outings have been reduced only to taking her to school.  The teacher and the psychologist at the institution suggest to Asuncian's mother some actions to follow:  Visual (photography) and verbal (short) anticipation before and after outings.  Positive reinforcement when the student shows the slightest expected behavior.  Considering Asuncian's characteristics, which of the following options most adequately justifies the relevance of the anticipation actions proposed by the professionals at this establishment?
options: They will provide her with security through social reinforcement
They will give her security through academic reinforcement
They will provide her with security through affective reinforcement
They will provide her with security through practicing her cognitive skills

question: The teacher is planning a class for a third year high school group, whose objective is "Identify centripetal acceleration and centripetal force as the causes of uniform circular motion". Which of the following prior knowledge about vectors must students necessarily have to recognize the effect of centripetal force on the motion of a body in uniform circular motion?
options: Subtraction of vectors
Cross product between vectors
Dot product between vectors
Decomposition of a vector into its Cartesian components

question: Read the following situation: Paulo is in 3rd grade and has an intellectual disability. His main challenges are in the area of Language and communication, especially in the areas of reading and writing, as he does not recognize his name written among several, identifies the grapheme of some vowels, and the initial sound of words when they begin with the letters he knows. He is interested in writing and his productions are at a pre-syllabic level. The teacher wants to start supporting his writing and has requested help from the special education teacher. From the constructivist methodology of written language, what are the strategies that support Paulo's acquisition of writing?
options: Work from his name, first identifying the letters he knows, such as a and p and then showing him other words, accompanied by images that have the same letters; give him opportunities to write his name correctly and progress in acquiring the consonants it contains.

Offer different opportunities to write his own name; provide a notebook for complementary work at home, with words presented clearly and of his interest, so he can make copies and sound out the letters he knows, along with presenting him the phoneme-grapheme correspondence of those he still does not master

Provide support from the special education teacher during different classes, to help him write simple words, dictating the letters he knows and using the alphabet with images attached to his desk; support his learning with worksheets where he works with the vowels he still does not master and then start with the consonants m, p, and l

Support processes of generalization of the vowels he knows, through the creation of literacy spaces (school and home), where posters with the names of the items present are placed; encourage writing by providing various opportunities to do so and ask him to write as he thinks is correct, valuing his creations and asking questions about them

question: According to Kohlberg's theory of moral development, which of the following options corresponds to a concept of the first stage of preconventional morality?
options: What is just is to follow the rule only when it benefits another
What is just is blind obedience to the rule, avoiding punishments
What is just is to fulfill duties that have been previously accepted before the group
What is just is to live according to what the people close to oneself expect

question: Read the following situation:  David is a student with mixed specific language disorder who attends 3rd grade in a school with an inclusion programme. He enjoys the subject of Technology, as he shows many manual skills and activities with sequential instructions facilitate his performance. This month the class started working on the unit: "Create technological objects", where they must design a work plan individually, select materials, and set a goal, but three classes have already passed and the student still cannot define a project, saying that he does not know how to start as everything seems difficult to him.  According to the situation presented, which of the following diversified strategies addresses the challenge that the student presents?
options: Use mentors to model the process of thinking aloud
Review guidelines together to identify short-term objectives
Allow other ways to present their projects such as using images
Provide self-assessment strategies to track their own progress

question: In a conversation with students from an 8th grade class, regarding the impact of technological solutions, a teacher introduces the concept of planned obsolescence (PO), commenting on the effects it has on the accumulation of technological waste. When discussing how to tackle this phenomenon, the students express skepticism and share various opinions about how difficult it is to reverse the general market trend of producing and selling in large quantities. Which of the following explanations from the teacher would clearly address the students' concerns and promote the skills expected to be developed in the subject?
options: “Indeed, the phenomenon of PO shows us that the development of technology has disadvantages at various levels and cannot be idealized without any questioning, or we will be causing irreversible environmental damage.”

“Since this is a global problem, it is clear that the commitment of all countries is required for its solution; therefore, we must be attentive to the different summits and conferences that will allow agreements also at a global level.”

“The market does not operate on its own, it responds to consumer demand, and this problem indicates that we must analyze and review our consumption habits to see how we are contributing to this vicious circle and how we could reverse it.”

“Hence the importance of societies being active promoters of change, as social movements are doing by publicly denouncing this problem so that authorities react, for example the SOP (Without Planned Obsolescence) movement.”

question: Read the following situation:  Martina is a four-year-old student attending first level of transition. She began to verbalize her first words around two and a half years, mainly to satisfy immediate needs. Currently, she maintains a peculiar jargon, which is sometimes characterized as unintelligible, even though her pronunciation is perfect when she verbalizes words she has heard on television. She also repeats some phrases from cartoons that she likes, imitating prosodic patterns that include the production of stress and tone of the heard pattern.  The characteristics of Martina's language have drawn the attention of her teacher, who wishes to conduct a psychopedagogical assessment to determine the origin of these characteristics, as she suspects the presence of an autism spectrum disorder in the student.  Which of the following procedures should the teacher carry out to conduct the psychopedagogical assessment of Martina?
options: Conduct an inventory of behaviors and skills aimed at identifying Martina's learning patterns and abilities in order to subsequently create guidelines and work strategies

Provide a guideline with a rating scale to the family in which the different areas of development are evaluated, and unify the resulting criteria from this evaluation with observations in the classroom
Request a psychometric assessment from the family to determine Martina's IQ, and based on the results, create a work structure that references those areas of development that present challenges

Prepare a checklist that evaluates the different objectives proposed in the Early Childhood Education Curriculum Framework to determine the areas that require support and define those that are satisfactorily achieved

question: Read the following classroom situation: One of the learning objectives in English for 11th grade is "to express oneself orally by means of monologues of about three minute extension and dialogues of about sixteen brief and simple exchanges that incorporate communicative functions of previous years and the function of informing what has been said by others, expressing a purpose". While evaluating this objective achievement, students in this level are asked to present a dialogue in groups of four in which they have to make use of a given set of vocabulary words, expressions and with a given purpose. Each time a group performs their dialogue, Mr. Fuentes, their English teacher, stops the conversation to correct grammar and/or pronunciation mistakes made by students. According to the specific evaluative orientations stated for this level study program, why would Mr. Fuentes not be acting in conformity to them?
options: Because today's learning theories state that oral expression is better developed within a relaxed environment
Due to the fact that interrupting a student’s speech for correction inhibits the communicative purpose of the task
Since foreign students would always make pronunciation mistakes and they would never eliminate them completely
Because making mistakes is part of every learning process and students should be given chances to express themselves freely

question: A teacher wants to evaluate through a written exercise if her students understand the consequences of the end of the Cold War. Which of the following requested tasks evaluates this objective?
options: Characterize the political transformations proposed by Glasnost in the USSR as one of the antecedents of the end of the Cold War
Describe the role played by Reagan and Gorbachev to bring an end to the Cold War
Identify one similarity and one difference between two historiographical approaches regarding the end of the Cold War
Explain what the geopolitical distribution of power was like after the end of the Cold War

question: Read the following situation:  Maria Ignacia is a 5th grade student at a school with an inclusion program. She has blindness due to advanced retinitis pigmentosa. Socio-emotionally, she demonstrates adequate interaction with her peers.  The classroom team, for the subject of Language and Communication, will evaluate the learning objective: "To write narratives clearly, creatively, and spontaneously." To this end, the special education teacher emphasizes the need to make access adjustments for Maria Ignacia.  Considering the above situation, which of the following adjustments is the most appropriate to assess Mari­a Ignacia's learning achievement?
options: Work with a tutor classmate
Develop her narrative in braille
Present her narrative orally
Complete the narrative that the teacher presents

question: A teacher is working with students on the multiplication of positive rational numbers. To do this, she plans a class in which the product between a natural number and a positive fraction will be addressed. According to her experience, one of the most frequent mistakes students make regarding this content is confusing the product of a natural number by a fraction with its amplification. For example, an exercise like 10 . 3/4 would be solved by them as 10 . 3/10 . 4. Due to this, the teacher decides to start the class by addressing this confusion.  Which of the following actions taken by the teacher allows students to understand the difference between both procedures?
options: Pictorially represent the fraction 3/4 ten times and then the fraction 30/40, comparing these representations to determine whether they are equivalent or not
Model a simple problem posed by the teacher using the operation 10 . 3/4 and another using the operation 10 . 3/10 . 4, developing both and comparing their results
Explain why the amplification of fractions is not equivalent to the multiplication between naturals and fractions, providing different examples that illustrate this difference
Transform the product of a natural number by a fraction into a fraction in which

question: A teacher is working with high school seniors on J. J. Rousseau's political theory, with a special emphasis on the idea of the general will, which was used by the French philosopher in his book The Social Contract (1762). Which of the following options represents a suitable example to illustrate the idea of the general will to these students?
options: Simple majority vote to decide the class leadership
Participation of all the students of the school and the teaching staff to reach an agreement
Request for academic reinforcement for the class, even though there are students who do not need it
Negotiation between students with opposing views to reach agreements within the class group

question: A teacher is planning a class for 5th grade with the following learning objective: "Characterize the landscapes of the large natural regions of Chile." Which of the following activities allows addressing the proposed objective?
options: Construct a model of the characteristic landscape of a natural zone highlighting the most well-known places, to exhibit it in the school yard
Research on the site www.sernatur.cl about the natural resources and economic activities associated with each zone, to present them in a diagram
Interview acquaintances who have been in another geographic zone, asking them about the landscapes and customs they saw, to create a travel journal
Create a tourist brochure with images of the landscapes promoting the geographical advantages and disadvantages of visiting each zone, to set up a poster exhibition

question: To start the unit Design and Dissemination, in a 2nd Year secondary class, a teacher conducts a diagnostic assessment to determine the students' approach to design and its different branches. Once the assessment is applied, the teacher identifies that they have a confused notion about industrial design, as they exclusively relate it to the manufacturing of industrial parts on a large scale.  What activity could the teacher propose to the students so they can understand the concept of industrial design?
options: Observe pieces of industrial design to analyze them, classify them, and make sketches of the exhibited pieces
Choose design pieces found in the classroom to investigate their origin, evolution, and relationship with their production context
Deconstruct an industrial design piece through photography, for the purpose of creating a new design piece that addresses current needs
Research iconic design pieces present in the history of industrial design, to subsequently create a design piece considering contextual needs

question: In the context of an activity of constructing an ecological brick, which of the following activities promotes metacognition in students after their construction?
options: Exchange ideas about the uses that can be given to an ecological brick
Create a photographic sequence of the process of building the ecological brick
Record a video about the use that can be given to the ecological brick once finished
Share the experience of making an ecological brick, recounting the challenges and learnings

question: In a Language class, to apply the concept of "propaganda", the teacher asks the students to create a campaign that promotes environmental care within the school. For this, various tasks are assigned, such as identifying the school's situation regarding environmental care, planning the campaign's objectives, formulating a creative strategy, and seeking the technical resources to carry out the project. To monitor the fulfillment of the first stage of the campaign, the teacher gives each group a checklist so they can self-evaluate. Why is it appropriate to use a checklist in this first stage of the campaign?
options: Because each student will be able to know how they are performing in the assigned tasks
Because the students will be able to categorize the activities they have completed
Because the students will be able to analyze in detail each of the actions they have carried out
Because the students will be able to verify if they have completed the proposed tasks related to the campaign

question: A Technology teacher for 2nd year high school, about to start a project work, decides to apply a diagnostic evaluation to their students to verify the knowledge and understanding of the stages of a project, which had already been covered the previous year. Considering the purpose and content to be evaluated, which of the following evaluation instruments is most appropriate?
options: A list of statements that collects the predominant ideas in the course about the different activities that are carried out in each stage of a project
A checklist that contains the quality indicators that must be present in the development of each stage of the project and that allows them to be verified in practical work
A written test with constructed response questions, in which, given a case, identify the different stages of a project and justify their answers
A performance rubric with the classification criteria of the level of mastery demonstrated over the stages of the project, as they develop each one of them in group work

question: A third-grade teacher evaluated, in the unit on Natural Resources Care, the objective "Recognize the Rule of the 3Rs in concrete actions". To do this, she posed an activity in which the students had to write an everyday action for each corresponding category: Reduce, Reuse, Recycle. One of the students submitted the following response:  1. Use washable masks. 2. Plant plants in butter containers. 3. Separate and sort the containers that will be thrown away at home.  What feedback from the teacher would allow the student to be improved and guided towards achieving the proposed objective?
options: Very good examples! But are all the Rs there? I invite you to answer these questions: What measure allows you to spend fewer resources or acquire only the essential? What new measure would you propose to complete your response?

Very good examples! But are all the Rs there? I invite you to write the definition of each of the 3Rs: Reduce, Reuse, Recycle, following the examples, and then answer this question: Which of these measures do you think is most effective in everyday life?

Very good examples! But you missed an R. To complete the answer, I invite you to answer these questions: What measures would you propose to implement at school? How would you motivate a classmate to find new uses for objects instead of discarding them?

Very good examples! But you missed an R. To complete the answer, I invite you to answer these questions: Why are these actions positive for the care of natural resources? To what extent do they contribute to reducing garbage and pollution on the planet?

question: A first-year high school teacher designs the following activity for her students: Take five cups with 50 mL of distilled water and dissolve the following in them: 5 g of salt (NaCl), 5 g of sugar, 5 g of bicarbonate, 5 mL of oil, 5 mL of alcohol. Then, the teacher asks them to explain why some of the added substances dissolve completely, others partially, and others do not dissolve. Which of the following objectives is consistent with this activity?
options: Compare the solubility of polar and non-polar substances in different solvents
Explain the behavior of different substances when dissolved in a non-polar solvent
Relate intermolecular interactions with the solubility of different substances in water
Classify the intermolecular interactions of substances according to their solubility in water

question: A teacher is working on active listening skills with her 5th grade students. To do this, she will present music of various formations and styles with the objective of having the students identify simple textures, such as melody and accompaniment, unison, polyphony, etc.  What musical resources, consistent with the developing skill and proposed objective, is it appropriate for the teacher to use?
options: Audio and score
Audio and musicogram
Video and musicogram
Video and score

question: Read the following situation:  Andrea is a student who attends 1st grade and is undergoing evaluation for a possible Learning Disorder. Her Language and Communication teacher indicates that the student faces challenges in reading and writing acquisition, mentioning that she cannot perform phoneme-grapheme association. Therefore, a psycho-pedagogical evaluation is requested to obtain information that can define the appropriate supports for the student. Considering the described situation, what evaluation procedure allows collecting the necessary information to determine the student's educational response?
options: Guide to associate different meanings with their signifiers
Test to know phonological awareness skills
Test to identify possible speech sound disorders
Dictation to verify recognition of vowels and consonants

question: A teacher tells his 3rd year high school students that during the unit "The Democratic Breakdown", the following objective will be worked on in groups: "Analyze the breakdown of democracy from a multi-causal perspective". Therefore, he proposes that they search for information about the period in different sources. Then, he explains that based on this information, in groups, they will share what they have investigated with the rest of the class. One of the groups asks the teacher if it is okay to make their presentation with a timeline of the 1970-1973 period, highlighting relevant and diverse historical events. The teacher, after hearing them, invites them to look for another form of presentation, as the proposed activity would not allow them to fulfill the selected learning objective. Why is the timeline resource inappropriate with respect to the learning objective proposed to the students?
options: Because it favors analysis from a chronological perspective, rather than from multiple perspectives
Because it distinguishes historical events, rather than establishing a hierarchy according to their importance
Because it limits the analysis to a linear view of events, rather than an understanding of the historical process
Because it makes it easy to order events, rather than grouping them according to the different dimensions involved in the historical process

question: In an 8th grade class, after finishing explaining the function of organelles in eukaryotic cells, a teacher is going to clarify their function using an analogy that relates machines and sectors of a factory to the cell.  Which of the following analogies that the teacher will mention corresponds to the cytoskeleton?
options: Machine that transforms organic matter into energy
Structure that allows for order to be maintained within the factory
Control center that has the information of the factory and directs what happens in it
Machine that marks products to be taken out of the factory

question: A 8th grade teacher is working with their students on the multiplication and division of rational numbers. During the class, a student points out the following: "When I multiply two positive integers, I always get a result greater than those numbers. Does the same happen when I multiply two positive rationals?" Which of the following explanations from the teacher precisely answers the student and, at the same time, facilitates the understanding of the content discussed in the class?
options: The set of rational numbers is an extension of the set of natural numbers; therefore, the product of positive rationals retains its properties. Let's develop as an example the product of \(\frac{7}{3} \cdot \frac{5}{2}\)

By definition, the result of multiplying positive numbers is always positive; therefore, the product of positive rationals also satisfies this property. Let's develop as an example the product of \(\frac{7}{2} \cdot \frac{5}{3}\)

The product of positive rationals is greater than its factors only if both are greater than 1; therefore, it is enough for one factor to be less than 1 for this to no longer hold. Let's develop as an example the product of \(\frac{3}{4} \cdot \frac{5}{2}\)

The pictorial representation of the product of two positive rationals corresponds to the graphical intersection of each of the factors; therefore, the result should be less than these. Let's develop as an example the product of \(\frac{3}{4} \cdot \frac{2}{5}\)

question: If a teaching team plans to promote the understanding and identification of relevant information from informative texts in 3rd grade students who have specific language disorders, what activity contributes to achieving the stated objective?
options: Conduct shared reading of the informative texts worked on in class
Supplement the reading with a video that conveys additional information
Attach a glossary of essential terms from the informative text to the reading
Incorporate subtitles that summarize and highlight the various subtopics of the text

question: In the unit "Interpreting urban music", a teacher asks the bassist of the course to transcribe for his instrument a score that was originally written for guitar. What prior knowledge must the student have to perform the requested transcription?
options: Reading bass clef notation
Reading treble clef
Reading American clef
Reading bass tablature

question: Which of these actions allows the use of a score editor as help for melodic transcription?
options: Identify dissonant turns within a score for multiple voices
Correct a musical phrase written based on its audition
Mute lines of a score for multiple voices, as a rehearsal tool in vocal/instrumental practice
Recognize motives or characteristic turns at the moment a musical piece is visualized and played

question: In the fluid mechanics unit of 3rd year, a teacher poses the following learning objective: "Explain various everyday phenomena using Bernoulli's principle." Regarding this, which of the following options corresponds to an indicator that would allow the teacher to know that the students have achieved the stated objective?
options: They explain the different terms of Bernoulli's equation
They explain how a perfume atomizer works
They explain why the diameter of a water jet decreases as it falls from a tap
They explain why a plastic bottle compresses when the air inside is sucked out

question: At the beginning of the first academic trimester, the educational team of the Senior Kindergarten level in a preschool is selecting educational resources to work with the children based on the following learning objective: "Progressively represent numbers and quantities in both concrete and pictorial form." Which of the following educational materials meets the purpose of this educational team?
options: Boxes with buttons: plastic boxes with buttons in various colors and sizes
Sandpaper numbers: cardboard cards of 15 x 15 cm, with numbers from 0 to 9 in sandpaper
Number activity sheets: laminated guides with exercises on numbers from 1 to 10. Letter-sized sheet
Number mat: foam mat, with numbers from 1 to 9, detachable. Each square approximately 30 x 30 cm

question: Read the following situation:  Agustin's teacher, a student with Autism Spectrum disorder in 1st grade, is working with the class on deepening positional value and mathematical reasoning, in relation to everyday contexts in which students use numbers. To do this, he wants to propose problems to the class involving counting in increments of 10 and 50, up to one hundred. For example, they must buy a fruit for a maximum price of 100 pesos and give or ask for change in coins of 10 and 50 pesos. To consolidate the understanding of this counting, prior to resolving problems that involve addition, the teacher invites the students to carry out the following exercise:  Place in the box the coins needed to complete 100 pesos:  Based on the above, why does the preparation strategy favor Agustin's learning?
options: It allows him to understand the new content by approaching it through visual aids
It allows him to gradually anticipate more complex mathematical exercises and avoid setbacks when facing the unknown
It allows him to internalize the reviewed content through the repetition of an exercise he will have to apply in problem solving
It allows him to put into practice his knowledge about transactions with coins in understanding mathematical concepts

question: In a summative assessment, an 8th-grade teacher has planned the following evaluation objective: "Explain the postulates of Dalton's atomic theory." Which of the following evaluation indicators would account for students achieving this objective?
options: They form different chemical compounds based on the choice of two or more elements
They determine the total number of atoms involved in a chemical reaction
They relate the mass of the elements in a compound to the total mass of the compound
They determine the mass of an element present in three different compounds of equal mass

question: A teacher in the first year of high school is working on a vocal piece, specifically a canon in unison for three voices. After explaining this musical procedure, he notices that his students have not fully understood it, so he proposes to use an analogy to facilitate their understanding. What of the following options presents an appropriate analogical comparison to help students understand an execution in unison canon?
options: Imagine that a relay race is being played and the baton is passed to the teammate at the precise moment, and then it is passed to the next one at determined times. The same happens with the melody to sing

Imagine that three people are reciting different poems simultaneously at the same speed, fitting and complementing each other, creating a new meaning for the text. In the case of music, the text should be replaced by sung melodies

Imagine several audio players with the same melody and then pressing play on each of them with a delay of a certain number of beats. In the case of sung music, each of those players would be a group of singers

Imagine that a drawing is made starting with a line in one color from the beginning of the page and then, a certain distance away, another line in a different color is drawn, and so on. The colored lines would be the melodies that should be sung and the centimeters of difference would be the waiting times

question: A teacher in the 2nd year of high school is working with his students on the interpretation of figurative language. To approach this learning, he is reading the following poem with his students:  TO WRITE  like a castaway like an exile like a nomad like a stateless person Keeping a distance from the power of money from vanity from cowardice Prefer the open air to enclosed spaces risk to security movement to safety Poetry is not a fixed place Poetry is a path A footprint in the dunes whose trace is followed knowing that it leads nowhere. (Jaime Pinos).  Which of the following instructions from the teacher guides the students in the interpretative reading task they should perform?
options: I recommend that they investigate the production context of the poem and what themes characterize the poet, and then the relationship with the presented poem
I advise them to first underline all the literary figures present in the text and then determine to which figures they correspond and, therefore, what is the correct way to read them
I suggest they look for all the words they do not understand from the poem, such as nomad, castaway, and stateless, and determine how they relate to one another

I suggest they look at the title first to determine what it is about and then start making associations between the key words that describe the subject, what those words literally mean, and the sensations they provoke

question: Read the following situation:  In 1st Grade of a school with special educational needs, some of the students are writing with a cart. Because of this, the teacher carries out an activity in which, after reading sentences of different lengths, they request them to identify the number of words contained in each one.  From the above, what is the reason for incorporating the type of activity described?
options: To develop lexical awareness with sentences
To understand the structure of written sentences
To increase reading comprehension of the sentences
To assimilate the functions of morphological particles in the sentence

question: Read the following situation:  Roberto is a blind student who has recently participated in a culinary workshop. Cooking has always fascinated him, and he would like to work in this field. However, he feels insecure about his ability to handle sharp utensils, which poses a challenge in his learning.  The workshop's special education teacher has set the following objective: Learn safety techniques for cutting and handling food to cook independently and safely. This is knowing that she must address and respond to Roberto's support needs.  Based on the above, what is the most appropriate strategy to address the proposed objective?
options: Use the hand-over-hand technique to chop vegetables, following instructions under task analysis
Use a finger protector, with instructions, under task analysis to chop vegetables and fruits on a cutting board
Follow oral instructions from an instructor to peel and chop vegetables without a cutting board, through tactile discrimination
Make small adjustments with silicone or foam rubber to mark the start of the blade and then cut the food on a cutting board

question: The high school students are in the planning stage of their project. To help them develop their plans, the teacher decides to dedicate a class to learning how to work with PERT diagrams, in which they must establish the dependency relationships of the activities for implementing their project. At the end of the class, the teacher decides to formatively assess the students' understanding of what these diagrams are and their usefulness, in order to adjust the planning of the next class based on the given responses. Which of the following instruments allows the teacher to quickly and concretely gather information about the students' understanding and the usefulness of PERT diagrams?
options: A peer evaluation form, in which students ask each other, “How to construct a PERT diagram,” and then co-correct their answers
An exit ticket, asking students to indicate three things they have learned from the class and two questions they still have about what PERT diagrams are and what they are useful for
A conceptual estimation scale, with categorization of the students' degree of understanding based on their responses to the question: “What is a PERT diagram?”

A questionnaire where students must answer what they have learned about what a PERT diagram is and what it is used for, according to the following categories: “I know it well; I know, but I cannot explain it; and I do not know”

question: In a 7th grade music class, the teacher is working on a three-part quodlibet. Once the three melodies are taught to the entire class, the teacher divides them into three groups, assigning one melody to each group. Upon superimposing the melodies, the teacher must stop the performance because one of the groups gets ahead and the quodlibet falls apart. What would be the best intervention on the part of the teacher to solve this problem?
options: Each group will perform its melody so that the rest of the groups can listen and understand the different parts of each in the working of the quodlibet
The melody will be performed together, but each group must focus on their voice to be internally aware of their part, so that no groups get ahead in the quodlibet entries
The groups that do not get ahead in the entries will perform their superimposed melodies, so the group that gets ahead can listen carefully to the functioning of the quodlibet while marking the pulse

The class will listen to examples of quodlibet to focus on one melody and then the next. The goal is for them to understand the interdependence of the voices and the importance of maintaining a steady pulse

question: Read the following situation:  Sofia is ten years old, is in 3rd grade, and has a learning disability. In the area of calculation, she manages to perform written operations with the support of pictorial material and understands basic mathematical concepts, showing needs for support in the writing of mathematical symbols. Her homeroom teacher is working in class on the skill of solving addition and subtraction problems.  According to this information, which of the following pedagogical actions promotes the development of mathematical problem-solving skills?
options: Suggest to the students that they propose situations in which they create mathematical problems
Verify with the students the operations and results of previously solved mathematical problems
Assign mathematical problems to groups of four students, who must perform the operations and write the result on the board
Allow different means for the students to explain the strategy used in solving a mathematical problem

question: A teacher of a 5th grade will evaluate the following learning objective: "Distinguish through listening the qualities of sound and elements of musical language, and represent them in different ways". Which activity proposed by the teacher allows assessing the stated objective?
options: The students listen to various musical works and then create a collage using cutouts of the emotions that arise from the music they listened to
The students listen to various pieces of music they personally like and then create a text that reflects the thoughts that arise from them
The students listen to music from their close environment and then, in groups, create a body expression scheme that manifests the emotions arising from their listening
The students listen to music several times while creating an abstract drawing of the different elements they perceive in it, through colors, shapes, and lines

question: A high school teacher is planning the unit on homotheties, their definitions and properties. Which of the following options represents the most appropriate sequence of contents for that purpose and level?
options: Location of points on the Cartesian plane. Distance between points on the Cartesian plane. Properties of homotheties on the Cartesian plane
Product between vectors and scalars. Homotheties given a figure and a factor. Properties of homotheties on the Cartesian plane
Distance between points on the Cartesian plane. Product between vectors and scalars. Homotheties given a figure and a factor
Homotheties given a figure and a factor. Distance between homothetic points. Problem-solving involving homotheties

question: In a 3rd year class, a secondary teacher is working on the following objective: "Recognize complex numbers as an extension of the numerical field of real numbers." In one of the exercises, a student presents the following procedure:   1 = √-1 - √-1 - √-1 - √-1 - i = i² = -1   Most students do not find the logical problem in the procedure.   Considering this evidence, what content did the students misunderstand, hindering the achievement of the stated objective?
options: The interpretation of the meaning of √-1
The application of the properties of roots and powers
The distinction between complex numbers that belong to the reals and those that do not
The understanding of the limitations of the properties of roots in complex numbers

question: A teacher needs to select a strategy to teach the application of a complex procedure, related to the use of electrical equipment. Primarily, she hopes her students learn to use the equipment with special emphasis on their own safety and that of others in its operation. Which of the following methodologies allows addressing this learning more directly?
options: Guide text
Case study
Guided demonstration
Problem solving

question: As part of the unit “Light and Geometric Optics,” a teacher needs to design an activity according to the following objective: “Critically analyze the corpuscular model of light to describe and explain luminous phenomena, understanding that models present advantages and at the same time limitations.” Which of the following activities will allow students to work on the stated objective?
options: Comparatively analyze the diffraction of light in a slit and its polarization when passing through a pair of polarizers, using the corpuscular model of light
Comparatively analyze the reflection of a light ray that reflects in a mirror and the diffraction of light in a double slit, using the corpuscular model of light
Comparatively analyze the refraction of a light ray that passes through a glass and the reflection of a light ray that reflects in a mirror, using the corpuscular model of light
Comparatively analyze the formation of images in curved mirrors and in concave lenses, using the principles of geometric optics, through the corpuscular model of light

question: A teacher introduces the concept of emotion and points out that emotions are intense, but they have a duration proportional to the stimulus that causes them. Next, she asks the students to make a list of emotions. A student suggests that, according to what the teacher explained, love is one of the emotions that should appear on the list. Which of the following statements by the teacher corrects the student's suggestion?
options: Love is such an intense emotion that the stimulus that produces it is always present
The state of being in love always requires the presence of the stimulus that causes it, so it is also an emotion
The state of being in love is not like an emotion, because it does not require the stimulus that causes it
Love does not involve the presence of the stimulus that causes it, so it is not necessary to consider it as an emotion

question: Read the following situation:  Daniel is a preschool student, diagnosed with mixed language disorder, who attends a language school. His educator has observed that he requires support to use definite articles in the noun phrase, as he makes concordance errors. This situation affects his interactions with peers and adults, as he finds it difficult to make himself understood.  Based on the above, which activity addresses the support needs of the student?
options: Observe and describe photographs, indicating what action is being performed by the subject in the image
Listen to and follow simple instructions given in group motor games
Recognize and name the actions performed by different participants in a miming activity
Associate and connect the determiner to the singular or plural noun in the provided sentences, as appropriate

question: In relation to the initiation to writing, which of the following educational situations is most suitable for promoting that children gradually produce their own graphic signs?
options: That they copy short messages to record the results of their activities
That they draw and write in their sketchbook every day on topics of their interest
That they have laminated cards to freely practice arabesques and garlands
That they complete sequences of dots in worksheets, respecting the start and end points

question: A teacher of a 3rd year high school class sets the goal for students to interpret music with stylistic awareness, technical precision, and fluency. To this end, she chooses a four-voice choral work to be performed by the entire course. During the process, she notices that while they sing fluently and there is an awareness of the work's style when interpreting it, they still do not articulate the text vocally in an appropriate manner while singing, therefore, the aspect of technical precision is not being achieved.  What comment from the teacher, as a form of formative feedback, would facilitate this learning for the students?
options: Very good interpretation considering the style of the work. However, in vocal music it is important that the text matches the corresponding rhythms, thus they will be able to articulate these aspects together

I congratulate you on your fluent interpretation of the piece. Now, it is important that, when interpreting music in voices, the singers listen to each other to achieve greater articulation among them

Well done so far; but there is still room for improvement in the interpretation of the work. To verify the correct articulation of the text, I recommend reviewing the reading of the score several times

Very well achieved fluency and interpretation of the work. However, to complete the process, it is important to reinforce the pronunciation of the text when singing. This will help to understand its meaning and make the musical execution more precise

question: A teacher of a 1st year high school class, during a session on environmental issues, must evaluate the following objective: "Understand the effects of emissions of chemical agents on the depletion of the ozone layer." Which activity would allow this teacher to collect evidence regarding the achievement of the proposed objective?
options: Ask students to draw conclusions from a table showing the concentrations of CFCs (chlorofluorocarbons) in the stratosphere and the concentration of ozone molecules there
Ask students to interpret the information from a graph that shows the variation in the concentration of ozone molecules in the stratosphere over the last five decades
Ask students to create a concept map that explains the consequences of the depletion of the ozone layer in the stratosphere
Ask students to design an infographic about household products that release CFCs (chlorofluorocarbons) into the ozone layer

question:
A 2nd-grade teacher set the class objective for her students to compare numbers from 0 to 100.
Which of the following performances would allow the teacher to assess if the students achieved the proposed objective?
options: Match different numbers less than 100 with their pictorial representation
Identify numbers that come before or after a given number less than 100
Decompose three consecutive numbers less than 100 into tens and units
Solve counting problems of numbers less than 100 using the number line

question: A teacher in 2nd year of high school will start working with the students on the content "inverse of a function". To plan their class, they will select a teaching resource so that the students achieve understanding of the content. Considering that in 8th grade the class learned about affine functions, in 1st year they represented linear relationships in two variables, and at this level they have already worked on objectives related to the quadratic function, which of the following resources is the most suitable for their class?
options: A sagittal diagram of the function \( f(x) = \sqrt{x+3} \) with different values, so that the students determine their preimage in each image and from that they can construct the definition of inverse function

A Cartesian plane with a quadratic function, specifying the coordinates of some points belonging to the function, so that the students understand the concept of inverse function based on algebraic verification

A Cartesian plane with a linear function from which the properties of injectivity and surjectivity can be explained, key concepts for understanding and applying inverse function, based on the graphical verification of these in the plane

A sagittal diagram of an affine function in which images are associated with each preimage, so that the students intuitively relate the given function with the function that associates these values in reverse, formulate conjectures, and formalize them together

question: Read the following situation:  Pablo is a 2nd year student of a special education establishment, who receives support due to the special educational needs arising from his diagnosis of intellectual disability. He presents significant challenges in transferring learning acquired from one situation to another. To respond to his support requirements, the classroom team decides to plan significant activities that take into account the immediate contexts in which Pablo operates, through which they can develop and assess the proposed work objectives in his Individualized Education Plan.  Why is it considered important to use the Functional Ecological Approach when developing activities and assessing Pablo's learning?
options: It allows for defining new forms of support in the planning and evaluation in the classroom
It allows for determining strategies that can be implemented from the student's home
It enables the identification of skills and support needs to participate in school, home, and community
It makes it possible to demonstrate the achievements and requirements presented in the teaching-learning process in school activities

question: Read the following situation: The students of a 3rd grade Basic class wrote stories to create an anthology. One of the difficulties they must overcome is the spelling in their writings. Which of the following strategies is most effective to address this problem?
options: Conduct a class using examples of the errors present in the stories
Implement moments of shared review and rewriting of the stories
Ask the students to work with a dictionary when writing
Ask the students to make flashcards with the difficult-to-spell words

question: In a high school class class, the teacher expects his students to learn how to solve quadratic equations using different methods. Which of the following activities will allow them to reinforce the basic ideas necessary to meet the proposed objective?
options: Reinforce notable products and factorizations of algebraic expressions
Reinforce notable products and review operations with powers and roots
Recall the properties of equality and apply them to solve linear equations
Factor algebraic expressions and recall under what conditions a product is equal to zero

question: A teacher of 8th grade, at the end of a unit, will evaluate her students' ability to select information sources, considering their reliability. To do this, she is preparing a guideline and has formulated the following evaluation indicators: 1. They identify the author of the source. 2. They briefly describe the origin or context of the source. Which of the following indicators, relevant to the content and level of the students, allows completing this evaluation guideline?
options: They identify the type of source
They argue about the meaning of the source
They recognize the intentionality of the source
